function.arguments:
{"new_string":"/**
 * An offset from UTC.
 *
 * This class represents the amount of time that a time zone differs from UTC at a particular moment in time.
 * It is always a fixed duration of time.
 *
 * [UtcOffset] can be acquired:
 * - By constructing it from components: `UtcOffset(hours = 2)`
 * - By parsing an ISO-8601 string: `UtcOffset.parse("+02:00")`
 * - From a [TimeZone] at a specific [Instant]: `timeZone.offsetAt(instant)`
 * - From the current system time zone offset: `TimeZone.currentSystemDefault().offsetAt(Clock.System.now())`
 *
 * Examples of these values:
 * - `Z`, an offset of zero;
 * - `+05`, plus five hours;
 * - `-02`, minus two hours;
 * - `+03:30`, plus three hours and thirty minutes;
 * - `+01:23:45`, plus one hour, 23 minutes, and 45 seconds.
 *
 * Basic usage example:
 * ```
 * val offset = UtcOffset(hours = 2)
 * val tz = offset.asTimeZone()
 * println("The offset is $offset")
 * ```
 */
@Serializable(with = UtcOffsetSerializer::class)
public expect class UtcOffset {","old_string":"/**
 * An offset from UTC.
 *
 * Examples of these values:
 * - `Z`, an offset of zero;
 * - `+05`, plus five hours;
 * - `-02`, minus two hours;
 * - `+03:30`, plus three hours and thirty minutes;
 * - `+01:23:45`, plus one hour, 23 minutes, and 45 seconds.
 */
@Serializable(with = UtcOffsetSerializer::class)
public expect class UtcOffset {","file_path":"core/common/src/UtcOffset.kt"}

1â†’/*
  2â†’ * Copyright 2019-2020 JetBrains s.r.o.

  4â†’ */
  5â†’
  6â†’package kotlinx.datetime
  7â†’
  8â†’import kotlinx.datetime.internal.*
  9â†’import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.DatePeriodIso8601Serializer
 10â†’import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.DateTimePeriodIso8601Serializer
 11â†’import kotlin.math.*
 12â†’import kotlin.time.Duration
 13â†’import kotlinx.serialization.Serializable
 14â†’
 15â†’/**
 16â†’ * A difference between two [instants][Instant], decomposed into date and time components.
 17â†’ *
 18â†’ * The date components are: [years], [months], [days].
 19â†’ *
 20â†’ * The time components are: [hours], [minutes], [seconds], [nanoseconds].
 21â†’ *
 22â†’ * A `DateTimePeriod` can be constructed using the same-named constructor function,
 23â†’ * [parsed][DateTimePeriod.parse] from a string, or returned as the result of instant arithmetic operations (see [Instant.periodUntil]).
 24â†’ * All these functions can return a [DatePeriod] value, which is a subtype of `DateTimePeriod`,
 25â†’ * a special case that only stores date components, if all time components of the result happen to be zero.
 26â†’ */
 27â†’@Serializable(with = DateTimePeriodIso8601Serializer::class)
 28â†’// TODO: could be error-prone without explicitly named params
 29â†’public sealed class DateTimePeriod {
 30â†’    internal abstract val totalMonths: Int
 31â†’
 32â†’    /**
 33â†’     * The number of calendar days.
 34â†’     *
 35â†’     * Note that a calendar day is not identical to 24 hours, see [DateTimeUnit.DayBased] for details.
 36â†’     */
 37â†’    public abstract val days: Int
 38â†’    internal abstract val totalNanoseconds: Long
 39â†’
 40â†’    /**
 41â†’     * The number of whole years.
 42â†’     */
 43â†’    public val years: Int get() = totalMonths / 12
 44â†’
 45â†’    /**
 46â†’     * The number of months in this period that don't form a whole year, so this value is always in `(-11..11)`.
 47â†’     */
 48â†’    public val months: Int get() = totalMonths % 12
 49â†’
 50â†’    /**
 51â†’     * The number of whole hours in this period.
 52â†’     */
 53â†’    public open val hours: Int get() = (totalNanoseconds / 3_600_000_000_000).toInt()
 54â†’
 55â†’    /**
 56â†’     * The number of whole minutes in this period that don't form a whole hour, so this value is always in `(-59..59)`.
 57â†’     */
 58â†’    public open val minutes: Int get() = ((totalNanoseconds % 3_600_000_000_000) / 60_000_000_000).toInt()
 59â†’
 60â†’    /**
 61â†’     * The number of whole seconds in this period that don't form a whole minute, so this value is always in `(-59..59)`.
 62â†’     */
 63â†’    public open val seconds: Int get() = ((totalNanoseconds % 60_000_000_000) / NANOS_PER_ONE).toInt()
 64â†’
 65â†’    /**
 66â†’     * The number of whole nanoseconds in this period that don't form a whole second, so this value is always in
 67â†’     * `(-999_999_999..999_999_999)`.
 68â†’     */
 69â†’    public open val nanoseconds: Int get() = (totalNanoseconds % NANOS_PER_ONE).toInt()
 70â†’
 71â†’    private fun allNonpositive() =
 72â†’        totalMonths <= 0 && days <= 0 && totalNanoseconds <= 0 && (totalMonths or days != 0 || totalNanoseconds != 0L)
 73â†’
 74â†’    /**
 75â†’     * Converts this period to the ISO-8601 string representation for durations.
 76â†’     *
 77â†’     * @see DateTimePeriod.parse
 78â†’     */
 79â†’    override fun toString(): String = buildString {
 80â†’        val sign = if (allNonpositive()) { append('-'); -1 } else 1
 81â†’        append('P')
 82â†’        if (years != 0) append(years * sign).append('Y')
 83â†’        if (months != 0) append(months * sign).append('M')
 84â†’        if (days != 0) append(days * sign).append('D')
 85â†’        var t = "T"
 86â†’        if (hours != 0) append(t).append(hours * sign).append('H').also { t = "" }
 87â†’        if (minutes != 0) append(t).append(minutes * sign).append('M').also { t = "" }
 88â†’        if (seconds or nanoseconds != 0) {
 89â†’            append(t)
 90â†’            append(when {
 91â†’                seconds != 0 -> seconds * sign
 92â†’                nanoseconds * sign < 0 -> "-0"
 93â†’                else -> "0"
 94â†’            })
 95â†’            if (nanoseconds != 0) append('.').append((nanoseconds.absoluteValue).toString().padStart(9, '0'))
 96â†’            append('S')
 97â†’        }
 98â†’
 99â†’        if (length == 1) append("0D")
100â†’    }
101â†’
102â†’    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean {
103â†’        if (this === other) return true
104â†’        if (other !is DateTimePeriod) return false
105â†’
106â†’        if (totalMonths != other.totalMonths) return false
107â†’        if (days != other.days) return false
108â†’        if (totalNanoseconds != other.totalNanoseconds) return false
109â†’
110â†’        return true
111â†’    }
112â†’
113â†’    override fun hashCode(): Int {
114â†’        var result = totalMonths
115â†’        result = 31 * result + days
116â†’        result = 31 * result + totalNanoseconds.hashCode()
117â†’        return result
118â†’    }
119â†’
120â†’    public companion object {
121â†’        /**
122â†’         * Parses a ISO-8601 duration string as a [DateTimePeriod].
123â†’         * If the time components are absent or equal to zero, returns a [DatePeriod].
124â†’         *
125â†’         * Additionally, we support the `W` signifier to represent weeks.
126â†’         *
127â†’         * Examples of durations in the ISO-8601 format:
128â†’         * - `P1Y40D` is one year and 40 days
129â†’         * - `-P1DT1H` is minus (one day and one hour)
130â†’         * - `P1DT-1H` is one day minus one hour
131â†’         * - `-PT0.000000001S` is minus one nanosecond
132â†’         *
133â†’         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the text cannot be parsed or the boundaries of [DateTimePeriod] are
134â†’         * exceeded.
135â†’         */
136â†’        public fun parse(text: String): DateTimePeriod {
137â†’            fun parseException(message: String, position: Int): Nothing =
138â†’                throw DateTimeFormatException("Parse error at char $position: $message")
139â†’            val START = 0
140â†’            val AFTER_P = 1
141â†’            val AFTER_YEAR = 2
142â†’            val AFTER_MONTH = 3
143â†’            val AFTER_WEEK = 4
144â†’            val AFTER_DAY = 5
145â†’            val AFTER_T = 6
146â†’            val AFTER_HOUR = 7
147â†’            val AFTER_MINUTE = 8
148â†’            val AFTER_SECOND_AND_NANO = 9
149â†’
150â†’            var state = START
151â†’            // next unread character
152â†’            var i = 0
153â†’            var sign = 1
154â†’            var years = 0
155â†’            var months = 0
156â†’            var weeks = 0
157â†’            var days = 0
158â†’            var hours = 0
159â†’            var minutes = 0
160â†’            var seconds = 0
161â†’            var nanoseconds = 0
162â†’            while (true) {
163â†’                if (i >= text.length) {
164â†’                    if (state == START)
165â†’                        parseException("Unexpected end of input; 'P' designator is required", i)
166â†’                    if (state == AFTER_T)
167â†’                        parseException("Unexpected end of input; at least one time component is required after 'T'", i)
168â†’                    val daysTotal = when (val n = days.toLong() + weeks * 7) {
169â†’                        in Int.MIN_VALUE..Int.MAX_VALUE -> n.toInt()
170â†’                        else -> parseException("The total number of days under 'D' and 'W' designators should fit into an Int", 0)
171â†’                    }
172â†’                    return DateTimePeriod(years, months, daysTotal, hours, minutes, seconds, nanoseconds.toLong())
173â†’                }
174â†’                if (state == START) {
175â†’                    if (i + 1 >= text.length && (text[i] == '+' || text[i] == '-'))
176â†’                        parseException("Unexpected end of string; 'P' designator is required", i)
177â†’                    when (text[i]) {
178â†’                        '+', '-' -> {
179â†’                            if (text[i] == '-')
180â†’                                sign = -1
181â†’                            if (text[i + 1] != 'P')
182â†’                                parseException("Expected 'P', got '${text[i + 1]}'", i + 1)
183â†’                            i += 2
184â†’                        }
185â†’                        'P' -> { i += 1 }
186â†’                        else -> parseException("Expected '+', '-', 'P', got '${text[i]}'", i)
187â†’                    }
188â†’                    state = AFTER_P
189â†’                    continue
190â†’                }
191â†’                var localSign = sign
192â†’                val iStart = i
193â†’                when (text[i]) {
194â†’                    '+', '-' -> {
195â†’                        if (text[i] == '-') localSign *= -1
196â†’                        i += 1
197â†’                        if (i >= text.length || text[i] !in '0'..'9')
198â†’                            parseException("A number expected after '${text[i]}'", i)
199â†’                    }
200â†’                    in '0'..'9' -> { }
201â†’                    'T' -> {
202â†’                        if (state >= AFTER_T)
203â†’                            parseException("Only one 'T' designator is allowed", i)
204â†’                        state = AFTER_T
205â†’                        i += 1
206â†’                        continue
207â†’                    }
208â†’                }
209â†’                var number = 0L
210â†’                while (i < text.length && text[i] in '0'..'9') {
211â†’                    try {
212â†’                        number = safeAdd(safeMultiply(number, 10), (text[i] - '0').toLong())
213â†’                    } catch (e: ArithmeticException) {
214â†’                        parseException("The number is too large", iStart)
215â†’                    }
216â†’                    i += 1
217â†’                }
218â†’                number *= localSign
219â†’                if (i == text.length)
220â†’                    parseException("Expected a designator after the numerical value", i)
221â†’                val wrongOrder = "Wrong component order: should be 'Y', 'M', 'W', 'D', then designator 'T', then 'H', 'M', 'S'"
222â†’                fun Long.toIntThrowing(component: Char): Int {
223â†’                    if (this < Int.MIN_VALUE || this > Int.MAX_VALUE)
224â†’                        parseException("Value $this does not fit into an Int, which is required for component '$component'", iStart)
225â†’                    return toInt()
226â†’                }
227â†’                when (text[i].uppercaseChar()) {
228â†’                    'Y' -> {
229â†’                        if (state >= AFTER_YEAR)
230â†’                            parseException(wrongOrder, i)
231â†’                        state = AFTER_YEAR
232â†’                        years = number.toIntThrowing('Y')
233â†’                    }
234â†’                    'M' -> {
235â†’                        if (state >= AFTER_T) {
236â†’                            // Minutes
237â†’                            if (state >= AFTER_MINUTE)
238â†’                                parseException(wrongOrder, i)
239â†’                            state = AFTER_MINUTE
240â†’                            minutes = number.toIntThrowing('M')
241â†’                        } else {
242â†’                            // Months
243â†’                            if (state >= AFTER_MONTH)
244â†’                                parseException(wrongOrder, i)
245â†’                            state = AFTER_MONTH
246â†’                            months = number.toIntThrowing('M')
247â†’                        }
248â†’                    }
249â†’                    'W' -> {
250â†’                        if (state >= AFTER_WEEK)
251â†’                            parseException(wrongOrder, i)
252â†’                        state = AFTER_WEEK
253â†’                        weeks = number.toIntThrowing('W')
254â†’                    }
255â†’                    'D' -> {
256â†’                        if (state >= AFTER_DAY)
257â†’                            parseException(wrongOrder, i)
258â†’                        state = AFTER_DAY
259â†’                        days = number.toIntThrowing('D')
260â†’                    }
261â†’                    'H' -> {
262â†’                        if (state >= AFTER_HOUR || state < AFTER_T)
263â†’                            parseException(wrongOrder, i)
264â†’                        state = AFTER_HOUR
265â†’                        hours = number.toIntThrowing('H')
266â†’                    }
267â†’                    'S' -> {
268â†’                        if (state >= AFTER_SECOND_AND_NANO || state < AFTER_T)
269â†’                            parseException(wrongOrder, i)
270â†’                        state = AFTER_SECOND_AND_NANO
271â†’                        seconds = number.toIntThrowing('S')
272â†’                    }
273â†’                    '.', ',' -> {
274â†’                        i += 1
275â†’                        if (i >= text.length)
276â†’                            parseException("Expected designator 'S' after ${text[i - 1]}", i)
277â†’                        val iStartFraction = i
278â†’                        while (i < text.length && text[i] in '0'..'9')
279â†’                            i += 1
280â†’                        val fractionLength = i - iStartFraction
281â†’                        if (fractionLength > 9)
282â†’                            parseException("Only the nanosecond fractions of a second are supported", iStartFraction)
283â†’                        val fractionalPart = text.substring(iStartFraction, i) + "0".repeat(9 - fractionLength)
284â†’                        nanoseconds = fractionalPart.toInt(10) * localSign
285â†’                        if (text[i] != 'S')
286â†’                            parseException("Expected the 'S' designator after a fraction", i)
287â†’                        if (state >= AFTER_SECOND_AND_NANO || state < AFTER_T)
288â†’                            parseException(wrongOrder, i)
289â†’                        state = AFTER_SECOND_AND_NANO
290â†’                        seconds = number.toIntThrowing('S')
291â†’                    }
292â†’                    else -> parseException("Expected a designator after the numerical value", i)
293â†’                }
294â†’                i += 1
295â†’           }
296â†’        }
297â†’    }
298â†’}
299â†’
300â†’/**
301â†’ * @suppress
302â†’ */
303â†’@Deprecated("Removed to support more idiomatic code. See https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx-datetime/issues/339", ReplaceWith("DateTimePeriod.parse(this)"), DeprecationLevel.WARNING)
304â†’public fun String.toDateTimePeriod(): DateTimePeriod = DateTimePeriod.parse(this)
305â†’
306â†’/**
307â†’ * A special case of [DateTimePeriod] that only stores date components and has all time components equal to zero.
308â†’ *
309â†’ * A `DatePeriod` is automatically returned from all constructor functions for [DateTimePeriod] if it turns out that
310â†’ * the time components are zero.
311â†’ *
312â†’ * `DatePeriod` values are used in operations on [LocalDates][LocalDate] and are returned from operations on [LocalDates][LocalDate],
313â†’ * but they also can be passed anywhere a [DateTimePeriod] is expected.
314â†’ */
315â†’@Serializable(with = DatePeriodIso8601Serializer::class)
316â†’public class DatePeriod internal constructor(
317â†’    internal override val totalMonths: Int,
318â†’    override val days: Int,
319â†’) : DateTimePeriod() {
320â†’    public constructor(years: Int = 0, months: Int = 0, days: Int = 0): this(totalMonths(years, months), days)
321â†’    // avoiding excessive computations
322â†’    /** The number of whole hours in this period. Always equal to zero. */
323â†’    override val hours: Int get() = 0
324â†’
325â†’    /** The number of whole minutes in this period. Always equal to zero. */
326â†’    override val minutes: Int get() = 0
327â†’
328â†’    /** The number of whole seconds in this period. Always equal to zero. */
329â†’    override val seconds: Int get() = 0
330â†’
331â†’    /** The number of nanoseconds in this period. Always equal to zero. */
332â†’    override val nanoseconds: Int get() = 0
333â†’    internal override val totalNanoseconds: Long get() = 0
334â†’
335â†’    public companion object {
336â†’        /**
337â†’         * Parses the ISO-8601 duration representation as a [DatePeriod].
338â†’         *
339â†’         * This function is equivalent to [DateTimePeriod.parse], but will fail if any of the time components are not
340â†’         * zero.
341â†’         *
342â†’         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the text cannot be parsed, the boundaries of [DatePeriod] are exceeded,
343â†’         * or any time components are not zero.
344â†’         *
345â†’         * @see DateTimePeriod.parse
346â†’         */
347â†’        public fun parse(text: String): DatePeriod =
348â†’            when (val period = DateTimePeriod.parse(text)) {
349â†’                is DatePeriod -> period
350â†’                else -> throw DateTimeFormatException("Period $period (parsed from string $text) is not date-based")
351â†’            }
352â†’    }
353â†’}
354â†’
355â†’/**
356â†’ * @suppress
357â†’ */
358â†’@Deprecated("Removed to support more idiomatic code. See https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx-datetime/issues/339", ReplaceWith("DatePeriod.parse(this)"), DeprecationLevel.WARNING)
359â†’public fun String.toDatePeriod(): DatePeriod = DatePeriod.parse(this)
360â†’
361â†’private class DateTimePeriodImpl(
362â†’    internal override val totalMonths: Int,
363â†’    override val days: Int,
364â†’    internal override val totalNanoseconds: Long,
365â†’) : DateTimePeriod()
366â†’
367â†’// TODO: these calculations fit in a JS Number. Possible to do an expect/actual here.
368â†’private fun totalMonths(years: Int, months: Int): Int =
369â†’    when (val totalMonths = years.toLong() * 12 + months.toLong()) {
370â†’        in Int.MIN_VALUE..Int.MAX_VALUE -> totalMonths.toInt()
371â†’        else -> throw IllegalArgumentException("The total number of months in $years years and $months months overflows an Int")
372â†’    }
373â†’
374â†’private fun totalNanoseconds(hours: Int, minutes: Int, seconds: Int, nanoseconds: Long): Long {
375â†’    val totalMinutes: Long = hours.toLong() * 60 + minutes
376â†’    // absolute value at most 61 * Int.MAX_VALUE
377â†’    val totalMinutesAsSeconds: Long = totalMinutes * 60
378â†’    // absolute value at most 61 * 60 * Int.MAX_VALUE < 64 * 64 * 2^31 = 2^43
379â†’    val minutesAndNanosecondsAsSeconds: Long = totalMinutesAsSeconds + nanoseconds / NANOS_PER_ONE
380â†’    // absolute value at most 2^43 + 2^63 / 10^9 < 2^43 + 2^34 < 2^44
381â†’    val totalSeconds = minutesAndNanosecondsAsSeconds + seconds
382â†’    // absolute value at most 2^44 + 2^31 < 2^45
383â†’    return try {
384â†’        multiplyAndAdd(totalSeconds, 1_000_000_000, nanoseconds % NANOS_PER_ONE)
385â†’    } catch (e: ArithmeticException) {
386â†’        throw IllegalArgumentException("The total number of nanoseconds in $hours hours, $minutes minutes, $seconds seconds, and $nanoseconds nanoseconds overflows a Long")
387â†’    }
388â†’}
389â†’
390â†’internal fun buildDateTimePeriod(totalMonths: Int = 0, days: Int = 0, totalNanoseconds: Long): DateTimePeriod =
391â†’    if (totalNanoseconds != 0L)
392â†’        DateTimePeriodImpl(totalMonths, days, totalNanoseconds)
393â†’    else
394â†’        DatePeriod(totalMonths, days)
395â†’
396â†’/**
397â†’ * Constructs a new [DateTimePeriod]. If all the time components are zero, returns a [DatePeriod].
398â†’ *
399â†’ * It is recommended to always explicitly name the arguments when constructing this manually,
400â†’ * like `DateTimePeriod(years = 1, months = 12)`.
401â†’ *
402â†’ * The passed numbers are not stored as is but are normalized instead for human readability, so, for example,
403â†’ * `DateTimePeriod(months = 24)` becomes `DateTimePeriod(years = 2)`.
404â†’ *
405â†’ * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the total number of months in [years] and [months] overflows an [Int].
406â†’ * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the total number of months in [hours], [minutes], [seconds] and [nanoseconds]
407â†’ * overflows a [Long].
408â†’ */
409â†’public fun DateTimePeriod(
410â†’    years: Int = 0,
411â†’    months: Int = 0,
412â†’    days: Int = 0,
413â†’    hours: Int = 0,
414â†’    minutes: Int = 0,
415â†’    seconds: Int = 0,
416â†’    nanoseconds: Long = 0
417â†’): DateTimePeriod = buildDateTimePeriod(totalMonths(years, months), days,
418â†’    totalNanoseconds(hours, minutes, seconds, nanoseconds))
419â†’
420â†’/**
421â†’ * Constructs a [DateTimePeriod] from a [Duration].
422â†’ *
423â†’ * If the duration value is too big to be represented as a [Long] number of nanoseconds,
424â†’ * the result will be [Long.MAX_VALUE] nanoseconds.
425â†’ */
426â†’// TODO: maybe it's more consistent to throw here on overflow?
427â†’public fun Duration.toDateTimePeriod(): DateTimePeriod = buildDateTimePeriod(totalNanoseconds = inWholeNanoseconds)
428â†’
429â†’/**
430â†’ * Adds two [DateTimePeriod] instances.
431â†’ *
432â†’ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if arithmetic overflow happens.
433â†’ */
434â†’public operator fun DateTimePeriod.plus(other: DateTimePeriod): DateTimePeriod = buildDateTimePeriod(
435â†’    safeAdd(totalMonths, other.totalMonths),
436â†’    safeAdd(days, other.days),
437â†’    safeAdd(totalNanoseconds, other.totalNanoseconds),
438â†’)
439â†’

{"old_string":"/**
 * A difference between two [instants][Instant], decomposed into date and time components.
 *
 * The date components are: [years], [months], [days].
 *
 * The time components are: [hours], [minutes], [seconds], [nanoseconds].
 *
 * A `DateTimePeriod` can be constructed using the same-named constructor function,
 * [parsed][DateTimePeriod.parse] from a string, or returned as the result of instant arithmetic operations (see [Instant.periodUntil]).
 * All these functions can return a [DatePeriod] value, which is a subtype of `DateTimePeriod`,
 * a special case that only stores date components, if all time components of the result happen to be zero.
 */
@Serializable(with = DateTimePeriodIso8601Serializer::class)
// TODO: could be error-prone without explicitly named params
public sealed class DateTimePeriod {","new_string":"/**
 * A difference between two [instants][Instant], decomposed into date and time components.
 *
 * The date components are: [years], [months], [days].
 *
 * The time components are: [hours], [minutes], [seconds], [nanoseconds].
 *
 * A `DateTimePeriod` can be used to represent a calendar-based duration, such as "1 month and 2 days".
 * This is different from a [Duration], which represents a fixed amount of physical time.
 * For example, adding "1 month" to a date depends on the month it is added to.
 *
 * A `DateTimePeriod` can be constructed using the same-named constructor function,
 * [parsed][DateTimePeriod.parse] from a string, or returned as the result of instant arithmetic operations (see [Instant.periodUntil]).
 * All these functions can return a [DatePeriod] value, which is a subtype of `DateTimePeriod`,
 * a special case that only stores date components, if all time components of the result happen to be zero.
 *
 * Example:
 * ```
 * val period = DateTimePeriod(months = 1, days = 2)
 * val nextEvent = lastEvent.plus(period, timeZone)
 * ```
 */
@Serializable(with = DateTimePeriodIso8601Serializer::class)
// TODO: could be error-prone without explicitly named params
public sealed class DateTimePeriod {","file_path":"core/common/src/DateTimePeriod.kt"}

1â†’/*

  6â†’package kotlinx.datetime
  7â†’
  8â†’import kotlinx.datetime.internal.safeMultiply
  9â†’import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.*
 10â†’import kotlinx.serialization.Serializable
 11â†’import kotlin.time.*
 12â†’import kotlin.time.Duration.Companion.nanoseconds
 13â†’
 14â†’/**
 15â†’ * A unit for measuring time.
 16â†’ *
 17â†’ * See the predefined constants for time units, like [DateTimeUnit.NANOSECOND], [DateTimeUnit.DAY],
 18â†’ * [DateTimeUnit.MONTH], and others.
 19â†’ *
 20â†’ * Two ways are provided to create custom [DateTimeUnit] instances:
 21â†’ * - By multiplying an existing unit on the right by an integer scalar: for example, `DateTimeUnit.NANOSECOND * 10`.
 22â†’ * - By constructing an instance manually with [TimeBased], [DayBased], or [MonthBased]: for example,
 23â†’ *   `TimeBased(nanoseconds = 10)`.
 24â†’ *
 25â†’ * Note that a calendar day is not considered identical to 24 hours. See [DateTimeUnit.DayBased] for a discussion.
 26â†’ */
 27â†’@Serializable(with = DateTimeUnitSerializer::class)
 28â†’public sealed class DateTimeUnit {
 29â†’
 30â†’    /** Produces a date-time unit that is a multiple of this unit times the specified integer [scalar] value. */
 31â†’    public abstract operator fun times(scalar: Int): DateTimeUnit
 32â†’
 33â†’    /**
 34â†’     * A date-time unit that has the precise time duration.
 35â†’     *
 36â†’     * Such units are independent of the time zone.
 37â†’     * Any such unit can be represented as some fixed number of nanoseconds.
 38â†’     */
 39â†’    @Serializable(with = TimeBasedDateTimeUnitSerializer::class)
 40â†’    public class TimeBased(
 41â†’        /**
 42â†’         * The length of this unit in nanoseconds.
 43â†’         */
 44â†’        public val nanoseconds: Long
 45â†’    ) : DateTimeUnit() {
 46â†’        private val unitName: String
 47â†’        private val unitScale: Long
 48â†’
 49â†’        init {
 50â†’            require(nanoseconds > 0) { "Unit duration must be positive, but was $nanoseconds ns." }
 51â†’            // find a concise string representation for the unit with this duration
 52â†’            when {
 53â†’                nanoseconds % 3600_000_000_000 == 0L -> {
 54â†’                    unitName = "HOUR"
 55â†’                    unitScale = nanoseconds / 3600_000_000_000
 56â†’                }
 57â†’                nanoseconds % 60_000_000_000 == 0L -> {
 58â†’                    unitName = "MINUTE"
 59â†’                    unitScale = nanoseconds / 60_000_000_000
 60â†’                }
 61â†’                nanoseconds % 1_000_000_000 == 0L -> {
 62â†’                    unitName = "SECOND"
 63â†’                    unitScale = nanoseconds / 1_000_000_000
 64â†’                }
 65â†’                nanoseconds % 1_000_000 == 0L -> {
 66â†’                    unitName = "MILLISECOND"
 67â†’                    unitScale = nanoseconds / 1_000_000
 68â†’                }
 69â†’                nanoseconds % 1_000 == 0L -> {
 70â†’                    unitName = "MICROSECOND"
 71â†’                    unitScale = nanoseconds / 1_000
 72â†’                }
 73â†’                else -> {
 74â†’                    unitName = "NANOSECOND"
 75â†’                    unitScale = nanoseconds
 76â†’                }
 77â†’            }
 78â†’        }
 79â†’
 80â†’        override fun times(scalar: Int): TimeBased = TimeBased(safeMultiply(nanoseconds, scalar.toLong()))
 81â†’
 82â†’        /**
 83â†’         * The length of this unit as a [Duration].
 84â†’         */
 85â†’        public val duration: Duration
 86â†’            get() = nanoseconds.nanoseconds
 87â†’
 88â†’        override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
 89â†’            this === other || (other is TimeBased && this.nanoseconds == other.nanoseconds)
 90â†’
 91â†’        override fun hashCode(): Int = nanoseconds.toInt() xor (nanoseconds shr Int.SIZE_BITS).toInt()
 92â†’
 93â†’        override fun toString(): String = formatToString(unitScale, unitName)
 94â†’    }
 95â†’
 96â†’    /**
 97â†’     * A date-time unit equal to some number of days or months.
 98â†’     *
 99â†’     * Operations involving `DateBased` units are performed on dates. The same operations on [Instants][Instant]
100â†’     * require a [TimeZone] to find the corresponding [LocalDateTimes][LocalDateTime] first to perform
101â†’     * the operation with the date component of these `LocalDateTime` values.
102â†’     */
103â†’    @Serializable(with = DateBasedDateTimeUnitSerializer::class)
104â†’    public sealed class DateBased : DateTimeUnit() {
105â†’        @Suppress("TOPLEVEL_TYPEALIASES_ONLY")
106â†’        @Deprecated("Use DateTimeUnit.DayBased", ReplaceWith("DateTimeUnit.DayBased", "kotlinx.datetime.DateTimeUnit"))
107â†’        public typealias DayBased = DateTimeUnit.DayBased
108â†’        @Suppress("TOPLEVEL_TYPEALIASES_ONLY")
109â†’        @Deprecated("Use DateTimeUnit.MonthBased", ReplaceWith("DateTimeUnit.MonthBased", "kotlinx.datetime.DateTimeUnit"))
110â†’        public typealias MonthBased = DateTimeUnit.MonthBased
111â†’    }
112â†’
113â†’    /**
114â†’     * A date-time unit equal to some number of calendar days.
115â†’     *
116â†’     * A calendar day is not considered identical to 24 hours, thus a `DayBased`-unit cannot be expressed as a multiple of some [TimeBased]-unit.
117â†’     *
118â†’     * The reason lies in time zone transitions, because of which some days can be 23 or 25 hours.
119â†’     * For example, we say that exactly a whole day has passed between `2019-10-27T02:59` and `2019-10-28T02:59`
120â†’     * in Berlin, despite the fact that the clocks were turned back one hour, so there are, in fact, 25 hours
121â†’     * between the two date-times.
122â†’     */
123â†’    @Serializable(with = DayBasedDateTimeUnitSerializer::class)
124â†’    public class DayBased(
125â†’        /**
126â†’         * The length of this unit in days.
127â†’         */
128â†’        public val days: Int
129â†’    ) : DateBased() {
130â†’        init {
131â†’            require(days > 0) { "Unit duration must be positive, but was $days days." }
132â†’        }
133â†’
134â†’        override fun times(scalar: Int): DateTimeUnit.DayBased = DateTimeUnit.DayBased(safeMultiply(days, scalar))
135â†’
136â†’        override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
137â†’            this === other || (other is DateTimeUnit.DayBased && this.days == other.days)
138â†’
139â†’        override fun hashCode(): Int = days xor 0x10000
140â†’
141â†’        override fun toString(): String = if (days % 7 == 0)
142â†’            formatToString(days / 7, "WEEK")
143â†’        else
144â†’            formatToString(days, "DAY")
145â†’    }
146â†’
147â†’    /**
148â†’     * A date-time unit equal to some number of months.
149â†’     *
150â†’     * Since different months have different number of days, a `MonthBased`-unit cannot be expressed a multiple of some [DayBased]-unit.
151â†’     */
152â†’    @Serializable(with = MonthBasedDateTimeUnitSerializer::class)
153â†’    public class MonthBased(
154â†’        /**
155â†’         * The length of this unit in months.
156â†’         */
157â†’        public val months: Int
158â†’    ) : DateBased() {
159â†’        init {
160â†’            require(months > 0) { "Unit duration must be positive, but was $months months." }
161â†’        }
162â†’
163â†’        override fun times(scalar: Int): DateTimeUnit.MonthBased = DateTimeUnit.MonthBased(safeMultiply(months, scalar))
164â†’
165â†’        override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
166â†’            this === other || (other is DateTimeUnit.MonthBased && this.months == other.months)
167â†’
168â†’        override fun hashCode(): Int = months xor 0x20000
169â†’
170â†’        override fun toString(): String = when {
171â†’            months % 12_00 == 0 -> formatToString(months / 12_00, "CENTURY")
172â†’            months % 12 == 0 -> formatToString(months / 12, "YEAR")
173â†’            months % 3 == 0 -> formatToString(months / 3, "QUARTER")
174â†’            else -> formatToString(months, "MONTH")
175â†’        }
176â†’    }
177â†’
178â†’    protected fun formatToString(value: Int, unit: String): String = if (value == 1) unit else "$value-$unit"
179â†’    protected fun formatToString(value: Long, unit: String): String = if (value == 1L) unit else "$value-$unit"
180â†’
181â†’    public companion object {
182â†’        /**
183â†’         * A nanosecond, which is `1/1_000_000_000` of a second.
184â†’         */
185â†’        public val NANOSECOND: TimeBased = TimeBased(nanoseconds = 1)
186â†’
187â†’        /**
188â†’         * A microsecond, which is `1/1_000_000` of a second, or `1_000` nanoseconds.
189â†’         */
190â†’        public val MICROSECOND: TimeBased = NANOSECOND * 1000
191â†’
192â†’        /**
193â†’         * A millisecond, which is `1/1_000` of a second, or `1_000_000` nanoseconds.
194â†’         */
195â†’        public val MILLISECOND: TimeBased = MICROSECOND * 1000
196â†’
197â†’        /**
198â†’         * A second.
199â†’         */
200â†’        public val SECOND: TimeBased = MILLISECOND * 1000
201â†’
202â†’        /**
203â†’         * A minute, which is 60 seconds.
204â†’         */
205â†’        public val MINUTE: TimeBased = SECOND * 60
206â†’
207â†’        /**
208â†’         * An hour, which is 60 minutes, or 3600 seconds.
209â†’         */
210â†’        public val HOUR: TimeBased = MINUTE * 60
211â†’
212â†’        /**
213â†’         * A calendar day.
214â†’         *
215â†’         * Note that a calendar day is not the same as 24 hours, see [DateTimeUnit.DayBased] for details.
216â†’         */
217â†’        public val DAY: DayBased = DayBased(days = 1)
218â†’
219â†’        /**
220â†’         * A week, which is 7 [calendar days][DAY].
221â†’         */
222â†’        public val WEEK: DayBased = DAY * 7
223â†’
224â†’        /**
225â†’         * A month.
226â†’         *
227â†’         * Note that a month doesn't have a constant number of calendar days in it.
228â†’         */
229â†’        public val MONTH: MonthBased = MonthBased(months = 1)
230â†’
231â†’        /**
232â†’         * A quarter, which is three [months][MONTH].
233â†’         */
234â†’        public val QUARTER: MonthBased = MONTH * 3
235â†’
236â†’        /**
237â†’         * A year, which is 12 [months][MONTH].
238â†’         */
239â†’        public val YEAR: MonthBased = MONTH * 12
240â†’
241â†’        /**
242â†’         * A century, which is 100 [years][YEAR], or 1200 [months][MONTH].
243â†’         */
244â†’        public val CENTURY: MonthBased = YEAR * 100
245â†’    }
246â†’}

{"new_string":"/**
 * A unit for measuring time.
 *
 * `DateTimeUnit` represents a unit of time that can be used in arithmetic operations on [Instant] and [LocalDate].
 *
 * There are three types of units:
 * - [TimeBased]: Units with a precise duration, such as [NANOSECOND], [SECOND], or [HOUR].
 * - [DayBased]: Units representing a number of calendar days, such as [DAY] or [WEEK].
 * - [MonthBased]: Units representing a number of months, such as [MONTH], [YEAR], or [CENTURY].
 *
 * See the predefined constants for time units, like [DateTimeUnit.NANOSECOND], [DateTimeUnit.DAY],
 * [DateTimeUnit.MONTH], and others.
 *
 * Two ways are provided to create custom [DateTimeUnit] instances:
 * - By multiplying an existing unit on the right by an integer scalar: for example, `DateTimeUnit.NANOSECOND * 10`.
 * - By constructing an instance manually with [TimeBased], [DayBased], or [MonthBased]: for example,
 *   `TimeBased(nanoseconds = 10)`.
 *
 * Note that a calendar day is not considered identical to 24 hours. See [DateTimeUnit.DayBased] for a discussion.
 *
 * Example:
 * ```
 * val twoDays = DateTimeUnit.DAY * 2
 * val nextMeeting = today.plus(twoDays)
 * ```
 */
@Serializable(with = DateTimeUnitSerializer::class)
public sealed class DateTimeUnit {","old_string":"/**
 * A unit for measuring time.
 *
 * See the predefined constants for time units, like [DateTimeUnit.NANOSECOND], [DateTimeUnit.DAY],
 * [DateTimeUnit.MONTH], and others.
 *
 * Two ways are provided to create custom [DateTimeUnit] instances:
 * - By multiplying an existing unit on the right by an integer scalar: for example, `DateTimeUnit.NANOSECOND * 10`.
 * - By constructing an instance manually with [TimeBased], [DayBased], or [MonthBased]: for example,
 *   `TimeBased(nanoseconds = 10)`.
 *
 * Note that a calendar day is not considered identical to 24 hours. See [DateTimeUnit.DayBased] for a discussion.
 */
@Serializable(with = DateTimeUnitSerializer::class)
public sealed class DateTimeUnit {","file_path":"core/common/src/DateTimeUnit.kt"}

1â†’/*

  6â†’package kotlinx.datetime.format
  7â†’
  8â†’import kotlinx.datetime.*
  9â†’import kotlinx.datetime.DayOfWeek
 10â†’import kotlinx.datetime.internal.*
 11â†’import kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.*
 12â†’import kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.parser.Copyable
 13â†’import kotlinx.datetime.internal.safeMultiply
 14â†’import kotlin.reflect.*
 15â†’
 16â†’/**
 17â†’ * A collection of date-time fields, used specifically for parsing and formatting.
 18â†’ *
 19â†’ * Its main purpose is to provide support for complex date-time formats that don't correspond to any of the standard
 20â†’ * entities in the library. For example, a format that includes only the month and the day of the month, but not the
 21â†’ * year, can not be represented and parsed as a [LocalDate], but it is valid for a [DateTimeComponents].
 22â†’ *
 23â†’ * Example:
 24â†’ * ```
 25â†’ * val input = "2020-03-16T23:59:59.999999999+03:00"
 26â†’ * val components = DateTimeComponents.Formats.ISO_DATE_TIME_OFFSET.parse(input)
 27â†’ * val localDateTime = components.toLocalDateTime() // LocalDateTime(2020, 3, 16, 23, 59, 59, 999_999_999)
 28â†’ * val instant = components.toInstantUsingOffset() // Instant.parse("2020-03-16T20:59:59.999999999Z")
 29â†’ * val offset = components.toUtcOffset() // UtcOffset(hours = 3)
 30â†’ * ```
 31â†’ *
 32â†’ * Another purpose is to support parsing and formatting data with out-of-bounds values. For example, parsing
 33â†’ * `23:59:60` as a [LocalTime] is not possible, but it is possible to parse it as a [DateTimeComponents], adjust the value by
 34â†’ * setting [second] to `59`, and then convert it to a [LocalTime] via [toLocalTime].
 35â†’ *
 36â†’ * Example:
 37â†’ * ```
 38â†’ * val input = "23:59:60"
 39â†’ * val extraDay: Boolean
 40â†’ * val time = DateTimeComponents.Format {
 41â†’ *   time(LocalTime.Formats.ISO)
 42â†’ * }.parse(input).apply {
 43â†’ *   if (hour == 23 && minute == 59 && second == 60) {
 44â†’ *     hour = 0; minute = 0; second = 0; extraDay = true
 45â†’ *   } else {
 46â†’ *     extraDay = false
 47â†’ *   }
 48â†’ * }.toLocalTime()
 49â†’ * ```
 50â†’ *
 51â†’ * Because this class has limited applications, constructing it directly is not possible.
 52â†’ * For formatting, use the [format] overload that accepts a lambda with a [DateTimeComponents] receiver.
 53â†’ *
 54â†’ * Example:
 55â†’ * ```
 56â†’ * // Mon, 16 Mar 2020 23:59:59 +0300
 57â†’ * DateTimeComponents.Formats.RFC_1123.format {
 58â†’ *    setDateTimeOffset(LocalDateTime(2020, 3, 16, 23, 59, 59, 999_999_999))
 59â†’ *    setDateTimeOffset(UtcOffset(hours = 3))
 60â†’ * }
 61â†’ * ```
 62â†’ *
 63â†’ * Accessing the fields of this class is not thread-safe.
 64â†’ * Make sure to apply proper synchronization if you are using a single instance from multiple threads.
 65â†’ */
 66â†’public class DateTimeComponents internal constructor(internal val contents: DateTimeComponentsContents = DateTimeComponentsContents()) {
 67â†’    public companion object {
 68â†’        /**
 69â†’         * Creates a [DateTimeFormat] for [DateTimeComponents] values using [DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDateTimeComponents].
 70â†’         *
 71â†’         * There is a collection of predefined formats in [DateTimeComponents.Formats].
 72â†’         *
 73â†’         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if parsing using this format is ambiguous.
 74â†’         */
 75â†’        @Suppress("FunctionName")
 76â†’        public fun Format(block: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDateTimeComponents.() -> Unit): DateTimeFormat<DateTimeComponents> {
 77â†’            val builder = DateTimeComponentsFormat.Builder(AppendableFormatStructure())
 78â†’            block(builder)
 79â†’            return DateTimeComponentsFormat(builder.build())
 80â†’        }
 81â†’    }
 82â†’
 83â†’    /**
 84â†’     * A collection of formats for parsing and formatting [DateTimeComponents] values.
 85â†’     *
 86â†’     * If predefined formats are not sufficient, use [DateTimeComponents.Format] to create a custom
 87â†’     * [kotlinx.datetime.format.DateTimeFormat] for [DateTimeComponents] values.
 88â†’     */
 89â†’    public object Formats {
 90â†’
 91â†’        /**
 92â†’         * ISO 8601 extended format for dates and times with UTC offset.
 93â†’         *
 94â†’         * For specifying the time zone offset, the format uses the [UtcOffset.Formats.ISO] format, except that during
 95â†’         * parsing, specifying the minutes is optional.
 96â†’         *
 97â†’         * This format differs from [LocalTime.Formats.ISO] in its time part in that
 98â†’         * specifying the seconds is *not* optional.
 99â†’         *
100â†’         * Examples of instants in the ISO 8601 format:
101â†’         * - `2020-08-30T18:43:00Z`
102â†’         * - `2020-08-30T18:43:00.50Z`
103â†’         * - `2020-08-30T18:43:00.123456789Z`
104â†’         * - `2020-08-30T18:40:00+03:00`
105â†’         * - `2020-08-30T18:40:00+03:30:20`
106â†’         * * `2020-01-01T23:59:59.123456789+01`
107â†’         * * `+12020-01-31T23:59:59Z`
108â†’         *
109â†’         * This format uses the local date, local time, and UTC offset fields of [DateTimeComponents].
110â†’         *
111â†’         * See ISO-8601-1:2019, 5.4.2.1b), excluding the format without the offset.
112â†’         *
113â†’         * Guaranteed to parse all strings that [Instant.toString] produces.
114â†’         */
115â†’        public val ISO_DATE_TIME_OFFSET: DateTimeFormat<DateTimeComponents> = Format {
116â†’            date(ISO_DATE)
117â†’            alternativeParsing({
118â†’                char('t')
119â†’            }) {
120â†’                char('T')
121â†’            }
122â†’            hour()
123â†’            char(':')
124â†’            minute()
125â†’            char(':')
126â†’            second()
127â†’            optional {
128â†’                char('.')
129â†’                secondFraction(1, 9)
130â†’            }
131â†’            alternativeParsing({
132â†’                offsetHours()
133â†’            }) {
134â†’                offset(UtcOffset.Formats.ISO)
135â†’            }
136â†’        }
137â†’
138â†’        /**
139â†’         * RFC 1123 format for dates and times with UTC offset.
140â†’         *
141â†’         * Examples of valid strings:
142â†’         * * `Mon, 30 Jun 2008 11:05:30 GMT`
143â†’         * * `Mon, 30 Jun 2008 11:05:30 -0300`
144â†’         * * `30 Jun 2008 11:05:30 UT`
145â†’         *
146â†’         * North American and military time zone abbreviations are not supported.
147â†’         */
148â†’        public val RFC_1123: DateTimeFormat<DateTimeComponents> = Format {
149â†’            alternativeParsing({
150â†’                // the day of week may be missing
151â†’            }) {
152â†’                dayOfWeek(DayOfWeekNames.ENGLISH_ABBREVIATED)
153â†’                chars(", ")
154â†’            }
155â†’            dayOfMonth(Padding.NONE)
156â†’            char(' ')
157â†’            monthName(MonthNames.ENGLISH_ABBREVIATED)
158â†’            char(' ')
159â†’            year()
160â†’            char(' ')
161â†’            hour()
162â†’            char(':')
163â†’            minute()
164â†’            optional {
165â†’                char(':')
166â†’                second()
167â†’            }
168â†’            chars(" ")
169â†’            alternativeParsing({
170â†’                chars("UT")
171â†’            }, {
172â†’                chars("Z")
173â†’            }) {
174â†’                optional("GMT") {
175â†’                    offset(UtcOffset.Formats.FOUR_DIGITS)
176â†’                }
177â†’            }
178â†’        }
179â†’    }
180â†’
181â†’    /**
182â†’     * Writes the contents of the specified [localTime] to this [DateTimeComponents].
183â†’     * The [localTime] is written to the [hour], [hourOfAmPm], [amPm], [minute], [second] and [nanosecond] fields.
184â†’     *
185â†’     * If any of the fields are already set, they will be overwritten.
186â†’     */
187â†’    public fun setTime(localTime: LocalTime) {
188â†’        contents.time.populateFrom(localTime)
189â†’    }
190â†’
191â†’    /**
192â†’     * Writes the contents of the specified [localDate] to this [DateTimeComponents].
193â†’     * The [localDate] is written to the [year], [monthNumber], [dayOfMonth], and [dayOfWeek] fields.
194â†’     *
195â†’     * If any of the fields are already set, they will be overwritten.
196â†’     */
197â†’    public fun setDate(localDate: LocalDate) {
198â†’        contents.date.populateFrom(localDate)
199â†’    }
200â†’
201â†’    /**
202â†’     * Writes the contents of the specified [localDateTime] to this [DateTimeComponents].
203â†’     * The [localDateTime] is written to the
204â†’     * [year], [monthNumber], [dayOfMonth], [dayOfWeek],
205â†’     * [hour], [hourOfAmPm], [amPm], [minute], [second] and [nanosecond] fields.
206â†’     *
207â†’     * If any of the fields are already set, they will be overwritten.
208â†’     */
209â†’    public fun setDateTime(localDateTime: LocalDateTime) {
210â†’        contents.date.populateFrom(localDateTime.date)
211â†’        contents.time.populateFrom(localDateTime.time)
212â†’    }
213â†’
214â†’    /**
215â†’     * Writes the contents of the specified [utcOffset] to this [DateTimeComponents].
216â†’     * The [utcOffset] is written to the [offsetHours], [offsetMinutesOfHour], [offsetSecondsOfMinute], and
217â†’     * [offsetIsNegative] fields.
218â†’     *
219â†’     * If any of the fields are already set, they will be overwritten.
220â†’     */
221â†’    public fun setOffset(utcOffset: UtcOffset) {
222â†’        contents.offset.populateFrom(utcOffset)
223â†’    }
224â†’
225â†’    /**
226â†’     * Writes the contents of the specified [instant] to this [DateTimeComponents].
227â†’     *
228â†’     * This method is almost always equivalent to the following code:
229â†’     * ```
230â†’     * setDateTime(instant.toLocalDateTime(offset))
231â†’     * setOffset(utcOffset)
232â†’     * ```
233â†’     * However, this also works for instants that are too large to be represented as a [LocalDateTime].
234â†’     *
235â†’     * If any of the fields are already set, they will be overwritten.
236â†’     */
237â†’    public fun setDateTimeOffset(instant: Instant, utcOffset: UtcOffset) {
238â†’        val smallerInstant = Instant.fromEpochSeconds(
239â†’            instant.epochSeconds % SECONDS_PER_10000_YEARS, instant.nanosecondsOfSecond
240â†’        )
241â†’        setDateTime(smallerInstant.toLocalDateTime(utcOffset))
242â†’        setOffset(utcOffset)
243â†’        year = year!! + ((instant.epochSeconds / SECONDS_PER_10000_YEARS) * 10000).toInt()
244â†’    }
245â†’
246â†’    /**
247â†’     * Writes the contents of the specified [localDateTime] and [utcOffset] to this [DateTimeComponents].
248â†’     *
249â†’     * A shortcut for calling [setDateTime] and [setOffset] separately.
250â†’     *
251â†’     * If [localDateTime] is obtained from an [Instant] using [LocalDateTime.toInstant], it is recommended to use
252â†’     * [setDateTimeOffset] that accepts an [Instant] directly.
253â†’     */
254â†’    public fun setDateTimeOffset(localDateTime: LocalDateTime, utcOffset: UtcOffset) {
255â†’        setDateTime(localDateTime)
256â†’        setOffset(utcOffset)
257â†’    }
258â†’
259â†’    /** The year component of the date. */
260â†’    public var year: Int? by contents.date::year
261â†’
262â†’    /**
263â†’     * The number-of-month (1..12) component of the date.
264â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException during assignment if the value is outside the `0..99` range.
265â†’     */
266â†’    public var monthNumber: Int? by TwoDigitNumber(contents.date::monthNumber)
267â†’
268â†’    /**
269â†’     * The month ([Month]) component of the date.
270â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException during getting if [monthNumber] is outside the `1..12` range.
271â†’     */
272â†’    public var month: Month?
273â†’        get() = monthNumber?.let { Month(it) }
274â†’        set(value) {
275â†’            monthNumber = value?.number
276â†’        }
277â†’
278â†’    /**
279â†’     * The day-of-month component of the date.
280â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException during assignment if the value is outside the `0..99` range.
281â†’     */
282â†’    public var dayOfMonth: Int? by TwoDigitNumber(contents.date::dayOfMonth)
283â†’
284â†’    /** The day-of-week component of the date. */
285â†’    public var dayOfWeek: DayOfWeek?
286â†’        get() = contents.date.isoDayOfWeek?.let { DayOfWeek(it) }
287â†’        set(value) {
288â†’            contents.date.isoDayOfWeek = value?.isoDayNumber
289â†’        }
290â†’    // /** Returns the day-of-year component of the date. */
291â†’    // public var dayOfYear: Int
292â†’
293â†’    /**
294â†’     * The hour-of-day (0..23) time component.
295â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException during assignment if the value is outside the `0..99` range.
296â†’     */
297â†’    public var hour: Int? by TwoDigitNumber(contents.time::hour)
298â†’
299â†’    /**
300â†’     * The 12-hour (1..12) time component.
301â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException during assignment if the value is outside the `0..99` range.
302â†’     * @see amPm
303â†’     */
304â†’    public var hourOfAmPm: Int? by TwoDigitNumber(contents.time::hourOfAmPm)
305â†’
306â†’    /**
307â†’     * The AM/PM state of the time component.
308â†’     * @see hourOfAmPm
309â†’     */
310â†’    public var amPm: AmPmMarker? by contents.time::amPm
311â†’
312â†’    /**
313â†’     * The minute-of-hour component.
314â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException during assignment if the value is outside the `0..99` range.
315â†’     */
316â†’    public var minute: Int? by TwoDigitNumber(contents.time::minute)
317â†’
318â†’    /**
319â†’     * The second-of-minute component.
320â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException during assignment if the value is outside the `0..99` range.
321â†’     */
322â†’    public var second: Int? by TwoDigitNumber(contents.time::second)
323â†’
324â†’    /**
325â†’     * The nanosecond-of-second component.
326â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException during assignment if the value is outside the `0..999_999_999` range.
327â†’     */
328â†’    public var nanosecond: Int?
329â†’        get() = contents.time.nanosecond
330â†’        set(value) {
331â†’            require(value == null || value in 0..999_999_999) {
332â†’                "Nanosecond must be in the range [0, 999_999_999]."
333â†’            }
334â†’            contents.time.nanosecond = value
335â†’        }
336â†’
337â†’    /** True if the offset is negative. */
338â†’    public var offsetIsNegative: Boolean? by contents.offset::isNegative
339â†’
340â†’    /**
341â†’     * The total amount of full hours in the UTC offset, in the range [0; 18].
342â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException during assignment if the value is outside the `0..99` range.
343â†’     */
344â†’    public var offsetHours: Int? by TwoDigitNumber(contents.offset::totalHoursAbs)
345â†’
346â†’    /**
347â†’     * The amount of minutes that don't add to a whole hour in the UTC offset, in the range [0; 59].
348â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException during assignment if the value is outside the `0..99` range.
349â†’     */
350â†’    public var offsetMinutesOfHour: Int? by TwoDigitNumber(contents.offset::minutesOfHour)
351â†’
352â†’    /**
353â†’     * The amount of seconds that don't add to a whole minute in the UTC offset, in the range [0; 59].
354â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException during assignment if the value is outside the `0..99` range.
355â†’     */
356â†’    public var offsetSecondsOfMinute: Int? by TwoDigitNumber(contents.offset::secondsOfMinute)
357â†’
358â†’    /** The timezone identifier, for example, "Europe/Berlin". */
359â†’    public var timeZoneId: String? by contents::timeZoneId
360â†’
361â†’    /**
362â†’     * Builds a [UtcOffset] from the fields in this [DateTimeComponents].
363â†’     *
364â†’     * This method uses the following fields:
365â†’     * * [offsetIsNegative] (default value is `false`)
366â†’     * * [offsetHours] (default value is 0)
367â†’     * * [offsetMinutesOfHour] (default value is 0)
368â†’     * * [offsetSecondsOfMinute] (default value is 0)
369â†’     *
370â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if any of the fields has an out-of-range value.
371â†’     */
372â†’    public fun toUtcOffset(): UtcOffset = contents.offset.toUtcOffset()
373â†’
374â†’    /**
375â†’     * Builds a [LocalDate] from the fields in this [DateTimeComponents].
376â†’     *
377â†’     * This method uses the following fields:
378â†’     * * [year]
379â†’     * * [monthNumber]
380â†’     * * [dayOfMonth]
381â†’     *
382â†’     * Also, [dayOfWeek] is checked for consistency with the other fields.
383â†’     *
384â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if any of the fields is missing or invalid.
385â†’     */
386â†’    public fun toLocalDate(): LocalDate = contents.date.toLocalDate()
387â†’
388â†’    /**
389â†’     * Builds a [LocalTime] from the fields in this [DateTimeComponents].
390â†’     *
391â†’     * This method uses the following fields:
392â†’     * * [hour], [hourOfAmPm], and [amPm]
393â†’     * * [minute]
394â†’     * * [second] (default value is 0)
395â†’     * * [nanosecond] (default value is 0)
396â†’     *
397â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if hours or minutes are not present, if any of the fields are invalid, or
398â†’     * [hourOfAmPm] and [amPm] are inconsistent with [hour].
399â†’     */
400â†’    public fun toLocalTime(): LocalTime = contents.time.toLocalTime()
401â†’
402â†’    /**
403â†’     * Builds a [LocalDateTime] from the fields in this [DateTimeComponents].
404â†’     *
405â†’     * This method uses the following fields:
406â†’     * * [year]
407â†’     * * [monthNumber]
408â†’     * * [dayOfMonth]
409â†’     * * [hour], [hourOfAmPm], and [amPm]
410â†’     * * [minute]
411â†’     * * [second] (default value is 0)
412â†’     * * [nanosecond] (default value is 0)
413â†’     *
414â†’     * Also, [dayOfWeek] is checked for consistency with the other fields.
415â†’     *
416â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if any of the required fields are not present,
417â†’     * any of the fields are invalid, or there's inconsistency.
418â†’     *
419â†’     * @see toLocalDate
420â†’     * @see toLocalTime
421â†’     */
422â†’    public fun toLocalDateTime(): LocalDateTime = toLocalDate().atTime(toLocalTime())
423â†’
424â†’    /**
425â†’     * Builds an [Instant] from the fields in this [DateTimeComponents].
426â†’     *
427â†’     * Uses the fields required for [toLocalDateTime] and [toUtcOffset].
428â†’     *
429â†’     * Almost always equivalent to `toLocalDateTime().toInstant(toUtcOffset())`, but also accounts for cases when
430â†’     * the year is outside the range representable by [LocalDate] but not outside the range representable by [Instant].
431â†’     *
432â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if any of the required fields are not present, out-of-range, or inconsistent
433â†’     * with one another.
434â†’     */
435â†’    public fun toInstantUsingOffset(): Instant {
436â†’        val offset = toUtcOffset()
437â†’        val time = toLocalTime()
438â†’        val truncatedDate = contents.date.copy()
439â†’        /**
440â†’         * 10_000 is a number that is both
441â†’         * * guaranteed to be representable as the number of years in a [LocalDate],
442â†’         * * and is a multiple of 400, which is the number of years in a leap cycle, which means that taking the
443â†’         *   remainder of the year after dividing by 40_000 will not change the leap year status of the year.
444â†’         */
445â†’        truncatedDate.year = requireParsedField(truncatedDate.year, "year") % 10_000
446â†’        val totalSeconds = try {
447â†’            val secDelta = safeMultiply((year!! / 10_000).toLong(), SECONDS_PER_10000_YEARS)
448â†’            val epochDays = truncatedDate.toLocalDate().toEpochDays().toLong()
449â†’            safeAdd(secDelta, epochDays * SECONDS_PER_DAY + time.toSecondOfDay() - offset.totalSeconds)
450â†’        } catch (e: ArithmeticException) {
451â†’            throw DateTimeFormatException("The parsed date is outside the range representable by Instant", e)
452â†’        }
453â†’        if (totalSeconds < Instant.MIN.epochSeconds || totalSeconds > Instant.MAX.epochSeconds)
454â†’            throw DateTimeFormatException("The parsed date is outside the range representable by Instant")
455â†’        return Instant.fromEpochSeconds(totalSeconds, nanosecond ?: 0)
456â†’    }
457â†’}
458â†’
459â†’/**
460â†’ * Uses this format to format an unstructured [DateTimeComponents].
461â†’ *
462â†’ * [block] is called on an initially-empty [DateTimeComponents] before formatting.
463â†’ *
464â†’ * Example:
465â†’ * ```
466â†’ * // Mon, 16 Mar 2020 23:59:59 +0300
467â†’ * DateTimeComponents.Formats.RFC_1123.format {
468â†’ *    setDateTime(LocalDateTime(2020, 3, 16, 23, 59, 59, 999_999_999))
469â†’ *    setOffset(UtcOffset(hours = 3))
470â†’ * }
471â†’ * ```
472â†’ *
473â†’ * @throws IllegalStateException if some values needed for the format are not present or can not be formatted:
474â†’ * for example, trying to format [DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.monthName] using a [DateTimeComponents.monthNumber]
475â†’ * value of 20.
476â†’ */
477â†’public fun DateTimeFormat<DateTimeComponents>.format(block: DateTimeComponents.() -> Unit): String =
478â†’    format(DateTimeComponents().apply { block() })
479â†’
480â†’/**
481â†’ * Parses a [DateTimeComponents] from [input] using the given format.
482â†’ * Equivalent to calling [DateTimeFormat.parse] on [format] with [input].
483â†’ *
484â†’ * [DateTimeComponents] does not perform any validation, so even invalid values may be parsed successfully if the string pattern
485â†’ * matches.
486â†’ *
487â†’ * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the text does not match the format.
488â†’ */
489â†’public fun DateTimeComponents.Companion.parse(
490â†’    input: CharSequence,
491â†’    format: DateTimeFormat<DateTimeComponents>
492â†’): DateTimeComponents =
493â†’    format.parse(input)
494â†’
495â†’internal class DateTimeComponentsContents internal constructor(
496â†’    val date: IncompleteLocalDate = IncompleteLocalDate(),
497â†’    val time: IncompleteLocalTime = IncompleteLocalTime(),
498â†’    val offset: IncompleteUtcOffset = IncompleteUtcOffset(),
499â†’    var timeZoneId: String? = null,
500â†’) : DateFieldContainer by date, TimeFieldContainer by time, UtcOffsetFieldContainer by offset,
501â†’    DateTimeFieldContainer, Copyable<DateTimeComponentsContents> {
502â†’    override fun copy(): DateTimeComponentsContents =
503â†’        DateTimeComponentsContents(date.copy(), time.copy(), offset.copy(), timeZoneId)
504â†’
505â†’    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
506â†’        other is DateTimeComponentsContents && other.date == date && other.time == time &&
507â†’            other.offset == offset && other.timeZoneId == timeZoneId
508â†’
509â†’    override fun hashCode(): Int =
510â†’        date.hashCode() xor time.hashCode() xor offset.hashCode() xor (timeZoneId?.hashCode() ?: 0)
511â†’}
512â†’
513â†’internal val timeZoneField = GenericFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(DateTimeComponentsContents::timeZoneId))
514â†’
515â†’internal class TimeZoneIdDirective(private val knownZones: Set<String>) :
516â†’    StringFieldFormatDirective<DateTimeComponentsContents>(timeZoneField, knownZones) {
517â†’
518â†’    override val builderRepresentation: String
519â†’        get() =
520â†’            "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDateTimeComponents::timeZoneId.name}()"
521â†’
522â†’    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean = other is TimeZoneIdDirective && other.knownZones == knownZones
523â†’    override fun hashCode(): Int = knownZones.hashCode()
524â†’}
525â†’
526â†’internal class DateTimeComponentsFormat(override val actualFormat: CachedFormatStructure<DateTimeComponentsContents>) :
527â†’    AbstractDateTimeFormat<DateTimeComponents, DateTimeComponentsContents>() {
528â†’    override fun intermediateFromValue(value: DateTimeComponents): DateTimeComponentsContents = value.contents
529â†’
530â†’    override fun valueFromIntermediate(intermediate: DateTimeComponentsContents): DateTimeComponents =
531â†’        DateTimeComponents(intermediate)
532â†’
533â†’    override val emptyIntermediate get() = emptyDateTimeComponentsContents
534â†’
535â†’    class Builder(override val actualBuilder: AppendableFormatStructure<DateTimeComponentsContents>) :
536â†’        AbstractDateTimeFormatBuilder<DateTimeComponentsContents, Builder>, AbstractWithDateTimeBuilder,
537â†’        AbstractWithOffsetBuilder, DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDateTimeComponents {
538â†’        override fun addFormatStructureForDateTime(structure: FormatStructure<DateTimeFieldContainer>) {
539â†’            actualBuilder.add(structure)
540â†’        }
541â†’
542â†’        override fun addFormatStructureForOffset(structure: FormatStructure<UtcOffsetFieldContainer>) {
543â†’            actualBuilder.add(structure)
544â†’        }
545â†’
546â†’        override fun timeZoneId() =
547â†’            actualBuilder.add(BasicFormatStructure(TimeZoneIdDirective(TimeZone.availableZoneIds)))
548â†’
549â†’        @Suppress("NO_ELSE_IN_WHEN")
550â†’        override fun dateTimeComponents(format: DateTimeFormat<DateTimeComponents>) = when (format) {
551â†’            is DateTimeComponentsFormat -> actualBuilder.add(format.actualFormat)
552â†’        }
553â†’
554â†’        override fun createEmpty(): Builder = Builder(AppendableFormatStructure())
555â†’    }
556â†’}
557â†’
558â†’private class TwoDigitNumber(private val reference: KMutableProperty0<Int?>) {
559â†’    operator fun getValue(thisRef: Any?, property: KProperty<*>) = reference.getValue(thisRef, property)
560â†’
561â†’    operator fun setValue(thisRef: Any?, property: KProperty<*>, value: Int?) {
562â†’        require(value === null || value in 0..99) { "${property.name} must be a two-digit number, got '$value'" }
563â†’        reference.setValue(thisRef, property, value)
564â†’    }
565â†’}
566â†’
567â†’private val emptyDateTimeComponentsContents = DateTimeComponentsContents()
568â†’

function.arguments:
{"new_string":"/**
 * A collection of date-time fields, used specifically for parsing and formatting.
 *
 * Its main purpose is to provide support for complex date-time formats that don't correspond to any of the standard
 * entities in the library. For example, a format that includes only the month and the day of the month, but not the
 * year, can not be represented and parsed as a [LocalDate], but it is valid for a [DateTimeComponents].
 *
 * `DateTimeComponents` is also useful when you need to parse a string that might contain out-of-bounds values,
 * or when you need to format data that doesn't fit into a single standard entity.
 *
 * How to acquire:
 * - By parsing a string: `DateTimeComponents.Formats.ISO_DATE_TIME_OFFSET.parse(input)`
 * - For formatting, it is provided as a receiver in the [format] lambda: `DateTimeComponents.Formats.RFC_1123.format { ... }`
 *
 * Example:
 * ```
 * val input = "2020-03-16T23:59:59.999999999+03:00"
 * val components = DateTimeComponents.Formats.ISO_DATE_TIME_OFFSET.parse(input)
 * val localDateTime = components.toLocalDateTime() // LocalDateTime(2020, 3, 16, 23, 59, 59, 999_999_999)
 * val instant = components.toInstantUsingOffset() // Instant.parse("2020-03-16T20:59:59.999999999Z")
 * val offset = components.toUtcOffset() // UtcOffset(hours = 3)
 * ```
 */
public class DateTimeComponents internal constructor(internal val contents: DateTimeComponentsContents = DateTimeComponentsContents()) {","old_string":"/**
 * A collection of date-time fields, used specifically for parsing and formatting.
 *
 * Its main purpose is to provide support for complex date-time formats that don't correspond to any of the standard
 * entities in the library. For example, a format that includes only the month and the day of the month, but not the
 * year, can not be represented and parsed as a [LocalDate], but it is valid for a [DateTimeComponents].
 *
 * Example:
 * ```
 * val input = "2020-03-16T23:59:59.999999999+03:00"
 * val components = DateTimeComponents.Formats.ISO_DATE_TIME_OFFSET.parse(input)
 * val localDateTime = components.toLocalDateTime() // LocalDateTime(2020, 3, 16, 23, 59, 59, 999_999_999)
 * val instant = components.toInstantUsingOffset() // Instant.parse("2020-03-16T20:59:59.999999999Z")
 * val offset = components.toUtcOffset() // UtcOffset(hours = 3)
 * ```
 *
 * Another purpose is to support parsing and formatting data with out-of-bounds values. For example, parsing
 * `23:59:60` as a [LocalTime] is not possible, but it is possible to parse it as a [DateTimeComponents], adjust the value by
 * setting [second] to `59`, and then convert it to a [LocalTime] via [toLocalTime].
 *
 * Example:
 * ```
 * val input = "23:59:60"
 * val extraDay: Boolean
 * val time = DateTimeComponents.Format {
 *   time(LocalTime.Formats.ISO)
 * }.parse(input).apply {
 *   if (hour == 23 && minute == 59 && second == 60) {
 *     hour = 0; minute = 0; second = 0; extraDay = true
 *   } else {
 *     extraDay = false
 *   }
 * }.toLocalTime()
 * ```
 *
 * Because this class has limited applications, constructing it directly is not possible.
 * For formatting, use the [format] overload that accepts a lambda with a [DateTimeComponents] receiver.
 *
 * Example:
 * ```
 * // Mon, 16 Mar 2020 23:59:59 +0300
 * DateTimeComponents.Formats.RFC_1123.format {
 *    setDateTimeOffset(LocalDateTime(2020, 3, 16, 23, 59, 59, 999_999_999))
 *    setDateTimeOffset(UtcOffset(hours = 3))
 * }
 * ```
 *
 * Accessing the fields of this class is not thread-safe.
 * Make sure to apply proper synchronization if you are using a single instance from multiple threads.
 */
public class DateTimeComponents internal constructor(internal val contents: DateTimeComponentsContents = DateTimeComponentsContents()) {","file_path":"core/common/src/format/DateTimeComponents.kt"}

  6â†’package kotlinx.datetime.format
  7â†’
  8â†’import kotlinx.datetime.*
  9â†’import kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.*
 10â†’import kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.parser.*
 11â†’
 12â†’/**
 13â†’ * A format for parsing and formatting date-time-related values.
 14â†’ */
 15â†’public sealed interface DateTimeFormat<T> {
 16â†’    /**
 17â†’     * Formats the given [value] into a string, using this format.
 18â†’     */
 19â†’    public fun format(value: T): String
 20â†’
 21â†’    /**
 22â†’     * Formats the given [value] into the given [appendable] using this format.
 23â†’     */
 24â†’    public fun <A : Appendable> formatTo(appendable: A, value: T): A
 25â†’
 26â†’    /**
 27â†’     * Parses the given [input] string as [T] using this format.
 28â†’     *
 29â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the input string is not in the expected format or the value is invalid.
 30â†’     */
 31â†’    public fun parse(input: CharSequence): T
 32â†’
 33â†’    /**
 34â†’     * Parses the given [input] string as [T] using this format.
 35â†’     *
 36â†’     * @return the parsed value, or `null` if the input string is not in the expected format or the value is invalid.
 37â†’     */
 38â†’    public fun parseOrNull(input: CharSequence): T?
 39â†’
 40â†’    public companion object {
 41â†’        /**
 42â†’         * Produces Kotlin code that, when pasted into a Kotlin source file, creates a [DateTimeFormat] instance that
 43â†’         * behaves identically to [format].
 44â†’         *
 45â†’         * The typical use case for this is to create a [DateTimeFormat] instance using a non-idiomatic approach and
 46â†’         * then convert it to a builder DSL.
 47â†’         */
 48â†’        public fun formatAsKotlinBuilderDsl(format: DateTimeFormat<*>): String = when (format) {
 49â†’            is AbstractDateTimeFormat<*, *> -> format.actualFormat.builderString(allFormatConstants)
 50â†’        }
 51â†’    }
 52â†’}
 53â†’
 54â†’/**
 55â†’ * The style of padding to use when formatting a value.
 56â†’ */
 57â†’public enum class Padding {
 58â†’    /**
 59â†’     * No padding.
 60â†’     */
 61â†’    NONE,
 62â†’
 63â†’    /**
 64â†’     * Pad with zeros.
 65â†’     */
 66â†’    ZERO,
 67â†’
 68â†’    /**
 69â†’     * Pad with spaces.
 70â†’     */
 71â†’    SPACE
 72â†’}
 73â†’
 74â†’internal fun Padding.toKotlinCode(): String = when (this) {
 75â†’    Padding.NONE -> "Padding.NONE"
 76â†’    Padding.ZERO -> "Padding.ZERO"
 77â†’    Padding.SPACE -> "Padding.SPACE"
 78â†’}
 79â†’
 80â†’internal inline fun Padding.minDigits(width: Int) = if (this == Padding.ZERO) width else 1
 81â†’internal inline fun Padding.spaces(width: Int) = if (this == Padding.SPACE) width else null
 82â†’
 83â†’/** [T] is the user-visible type, whereas [U] is its mutable representation for parsing and formatting. */
 84â†’internal sealed class AbstractDateTimeFormat<T, U : Copyable<U>> : DateTimeFormat<T> {
 85â†’
 86â†’    abstract val actualFormat: CachedFormatStructure<U>
 87â†’
 88â†’    abstract fun intermediateFromValue(value: T): U
 89â†’
 90â†’    abstract fun valueFromIntermediate(intermediate: U): T
 91â†’
 92â†’    abstract val emptyIntermediate: U // should be part of the `Copyable` interface once the language allows this
 93â†’
 94â†’    open fun valueFromIntermediateOrNull(intermediate: U): T? = try {
 95â†’        valueFromIntermediate(intermediate)
 96â†’    } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
 97â†’        null
 98â†’    }
 99â†’
100â†’    override fun format(value: T): String = StringBuilder().also {
101â†’        actualFormat.formatter().format(intermediateFromValue(value), it)
102â†’    }.toString()
103â†’
104â†’    override fun <A : Appendable> formatTo(appendable: A, value: T): A = appendable.apply {
105â†’        actualFormat.formatter().format(intermediateFromValue(value), this)
106â†’    }
107â†’
108â†’    override fun parse(input: CharSequence): T {
109â†’        val matched = try {
110â†’            // without the fully qualified name, the compilation fails for some reason
111â†’            Parser(actualFormat.parser()).match(input, emptyIntermediate)
112â†’        } catch (e: ParseException) {
113â†’            throw DateTimeFormatException("Failed to parse value from '$input'", e)
114â†’        }
115â†’        try {
116â†’            return valueFromIntermediate(matched)
117â†’        } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
118â†’            throw DateTimeFormatException(when (val message = e.message) {
119â†’                null -> "The value parsed from '$input' is invalid"
120â†’                else -> "$message (when parsing '$input')"
121â†’            }, e)
122â†’        }
123â†’    }
124â†’
125â†’    override fun parseOrNull(input: CharSequence): T? =
126â†’        // without the fully qualified name, the compilation fails for some reason
127â†’        Parser(actualFormat.parser()).matchOrNull(input, emptyIntermediate)?.let { valueFromIntermediateOrNull(it) }
128â†’
129â†’}
130â†’
131â†’private val allFormatConstants: List<Pair<String, CachedFormatStructure<*>>> by lazy {
132â†’    fun unwrap(format: DateTimeFormat<*>): CachedFormatStructure<*> = (format as AbstractDateTimeFormat<*, *>).actualFormat
133â†’    // the formats are ordered vaguely by decreasing length, as the topmost among suitable ones is chosen.
134â†’    listOf(
135â†’        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDateTimeComponents::dateTimeComponents.name}(DateTimeComponents.Formats.RFC_1123)" to
136â†’            unwrap(DateTimeComponents.Formats.RFC_1123),
137â†’        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDateTimeComponents::dateTimeComponents.name}(DateTimeComponents.Formats.ISO_DATE_TIME_OFFSET)" to
138â†’            unwrap(DateTimeComponents.Formats.ISO_DATE_TIME_OFFSET),
139â†’        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDateTime::date.name}(LocalDateTime.Formats.ISO)" to unwrap(LocalDateTime.Formats.ISO),
140â†’        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::date.name}(LocalDate.Formats.ISO)" to unwrap(LocalDate.Formats.ISO),
141â†’        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::date.name}(LocalDate.Formats.ISO_BASIC)" to unwrap(LocalDate.Formats.ISO_BASIC),
142â†’        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime::time.name}(LocalTime.Formats.ISO)" to unwrap(LocalTime.Formats.ISO),
143â†’        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithUtcOffset::offset.name}(UtcOffset.Formats.ISO)" to unwrap(UtcOffset.Formats.ISO),
144â†’        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithUtcOffset::offset.name}(UtcOffset.Formats.ISO_BASIC)" to unwrap(UtcOffset.Formats.ISO_BASIC),
145â†’        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithUtcOffset::offset.name}(UtcOffset.Formats.FOUR_DIGITS)" to unwrap(UtcOffset.Formats.FOUR_DIGITS),
146â†’    )
147â†’}
148â†’

function.arguments:
{"file_path":"core/common/src/format/DateTimeFormat.kt","new_string":"/**
 * A format for parsing and formatting date-time-related values.
 *
 * This interface is implemented by formats for [LocalDate], [LocalTime], [LocalDateTime], [UtcOffset],
 * and [DateTimeComponents].
 *
 * You can use predefined formats like [LocalDate.Formats.ISO] or create custom formats using the `Format { ... }`
 * DSL provided by the respective classes.
 *
 * Example:
 * ```
 * val format = LocalDate.Format {
 *     year()
 *     char('-')
 *     monthNumber()
 *     char('-')
 *     dayOfMonth()
 * }
 * val dateString = format.format(LocalDate(2023, 12, 25)) // "2023-12-25"
 * val date = format.parse("2023-12-25")
 * ```
 *
 * @param T the type of value that this format can parse and format.
 */
public sealed interface DateTimeFormat<T> {","old_string":"/**
 * A format for parsing and formatting date-time-related values.
 */
public sealed interface DateTimeFormat<T> {"}

1â†’/*
