  5â†’
  6â†’package kotlinx.datetime.format
  7â†’
  8â†’import kotlinx.datetime.*
  9â†’import kotlinx.datetime.internal.*
 10â†’import kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.*
 11â†’
 12â†’/**
 13â†’ * Common functions for all format builders.
 14â†’ */
 15â†’public sealed interface DateTimeFormatBuilder {
 16â†’    /**
 17â†’     * A literal string.
 18â†’     *
 19â†’     * When formatting, the string is appended to the result as is,
 20â†’     * and when parsing, the string is expected to be present in the input verbatim.
 21â†’     */
 22â†’    public fun chars(value: String)
 23â†’
 24â†’    /**
 25â†’     * Functions specific to the date-time format builders containing the local-date fields.
 26â†’     */
 27â†’    public sealed interface WithDate : DateTimeFormatBuilder {
 28â†’        /**
 29â†’         * A year number.
 30â†’         *
 31â†’         * By default, for years [-9999..9999], it's formatted as a decimal number, zero-padded to four digits, though
 32â†’         * this padding can be disabled or changed to space padding by passing [padding].
 33â†’         * For years outside this range, it's formatted as a decimal number with a leading sign, so the year 12345
 34â†’         * is formatted as "+12345".
 35â†’         */
 36â†’        public fun year(padding: Padding = Padding.ZERO)
 37â†’
 38â†’        /**
 39â†’         * The last two digits of the ISO year.
 40â†’         *
 41â†’         * [baseYear] is the base year for the two-digit year.
 42â†’         * For example, if [baseYear] is 1960, then this format correctly works with years [1960..2059].
 43â†’         *
 44â†’         * On formatting, when given a year in the valid range, it returns the last two digits of the year,
 45â†’         * so 1993 becomes "93". When given a year outside the valid range, it returns the full year number
 46â†’         * with a leading sign, so 1850 becomes "+1850", and -200 becomes "-200".
 47â†’         *
 48â†’         * On parsing, it accepts either a two-digit year or a full year number with a leading sign.
 49â†’         * When given a two-digit year, it returns a year in the valid range, so "93" becomes 1993,
 50â†’         * and when given a full year number with a leading sign, it parses the full year number,
 51â†’         * so "+1850" becomes 1850.
 52â†’         */
 53â†’        public fun yearTwoDigits(baseYear: Int)
 54â†’
 55â†’        /**
 56â†’         * A month-of-year number, from 1 to 12.
 57â†’         *
 58â†’         * By default, it's padded with zeros to two digits. This can be changed by passing [padding].
 59â†’         */
 60â†’        public fun monthNumber(padding: Padding = Padding.ZERO)
 61â†’
 62â†’        /**
 63â†’         * A month name (for example, "January").
 64â†’         *
 65â†’         * Example:
 66â†’         * ```
 67â†’         * monthName(MonthNames.ENGLISH_FULL)
 68â†’         * ```
 69â†’         */
 70â†’        public fun monthName(names: MonthNames)
 71â†’
 72â†’        /**
 73â†’         * A day-of-month number, from 1 to 31.
 74â†’         *
 75â†’         * By default, it's padded with zeros to two digits. This can be changed by passing [padding].
 76â†’         */
 77â†’        public fun dayOfMonth(padding: Padding = Padding.ZERO)
 78â†’
 79â†’        /**
 80â†’         * A day-of-week name (for example, "Thursday").
 81â†’         *
 82â†’         * Example:
 83â†’         * ```
 84â†’         * dayOfWeek(DayOfWeekNames.ENGLISH_FULL)
 85â†’         * ```
 86â†’         */
 87â†’        public fun dayOfWeek(names: DayOfWeekNames)
 88â†’
 89â†’        /**
 90â†’         * An existing [DateTimeFormat] for the date part.
 91â†’         *
 92â†’         * Example:
 93â†’         * ```
 94â†’         * date(LocalDate.Formats.ISO)
 95â†’         * ```
 96â†’         */
 97â†’        public fun date(format: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate>)
 98â†’    }
 99â†’
100â†’    /**
101â†’     * Functions specific to the date-time format builders containing the local-time fields.
102â†’     */
103â†’    public sealed interface WithTime : DateTimeFormatBuilder {
104â†’        /**
105â†’         * The hour of the day, from 0 to 23.
106â†’         *
107â†’         * By default, it's zero-padded to two digits, but this can be changed with [padding].
108â†’         */
109â†’        public fun hour(padding: Padding = Padding.ZERO)
110â†’
111â†’        /**
112â†’         * The hour of the day in the 12-hour clock:
113â†’         *
114â†’         * * Midnight is 12,
115â†’         * * Hours 1-11 are 1-11,
116â†’         * * Noon is 12,
117â†’         * * Hours 13-23 are 1-11.
118â†’         *
119â†’         * To disambiguate between the first and the second halves of the day, [amPmMarker] should be used.
120â†’         *
121â†’         * By default, it's zero-padded to two digits, but this can be changed with [padding].
122â†’         *
123â†’         * @see [amPmMarker]
124â†’         */
125â†’        public fun amPmHour(padding: Padding = Padding.ZERO)
126â†’
127â†’        /**
128â†’         * The AM/PM marker, using the specified strings.
129â†’         *
130â†’         * [am] is used for the AM marker (0-11 hours), [pm] is used for the PM marker (12-23 hours).
131â†’         *
132â†’         * Empty strings can not be used as markers.
133â†’         * [IllegalArgumentException] is thrown if either [am] or [pm] is empty or if they are equal.
134â†’         *
135â†’         * @see [amPmHour]
136â†’         */
137â†’        public fun amPmMarker(am: String, pm: String)
138â†’
139â†’        /**
140â†’         * The minute of hour.
141â†’         *
142â†’         * By default, it's zero-padded to two digits, but this can be changed with [padding].
143â†’         */
144â†’        public fun minute(padding: Padding = Padding.ZERO)
145â†’
146â†’        /**
147â†’         * The second of minute.
148â†’         *
149â†’         * By default, it's zero-padded to two digits, but this can be changed with [padding].
150â†’         *
151â†’         * This field has the default value of 0. If you want to omit it, use [optional].
152â†’         */
153â†’        public fun second(padding: Padding = Padding.ZERO)
154â†’
155â†’        /**
156â†’         * The fractional part of the second without the leading dot.
157â†’         *
158â†’         * When formatting, the decimal fraction will be rounded to fit in the specified [maxLength] and will add
159â†’         * trailing zeroes to the specified [minLength].
160â†’         * Rounding is performed using the round-toward-zero rounding mode.
161â†’         *
162â†’         * When parsing, the parser will require that the fraction is at least [minLength] and at most [maxLength]
163â†’         * digits long.
164â†’         *
165â†’         * This field has the default value of 0. If you want to omit it, use [optional].
166â†’         *
167â†’         * See also the [secondFraction] overload that accepts just one parameter, the exact length of the fractional
168â†’         * part.
169â†’         *
170â†’         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if [minLength] is greater than [maxLength] or if either is not in the range 1..9.
171â†’         */
172â†’        public fun secondFraction(minLength: Int = 1, maxLength: Int = 9)
173â†’
174â†’        /**
175â†’         * The fractional part of the second without the leading dot.
176â†’         *
177â†’         * When formatting, the decimal fraction will add trailing zeroes or be rounded as necessary to always output
178â†’         * exactly the number of digits specified in [fixedLength].
179â†’         * Rounding is performed using the round-toward-zero rounding mode.
180â†’         *
181â†’         * When parsing, exactly [fixedLength] digits will be consumed.
182â†’         *
183â†’         * This field has the default value of 0. If you want to omit it, use [optional].
184â†’         *
185â†’         * See also the [secondFraction] overload that accepts two parameters, the minimum and maximum length of the
186â†’         * fractional part.
187â†’         *
188â†’         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if [fixedLength] is not in the range 1..9.
189â†’         *
190â†’         * @see secondFraction that accepts two parameters.
191â†’         */
192â†’        public fun secondFraction(fixedLength: Int) {
193â†’            secondFraction(fixedLength, fixedLength)
194â†’        }
195â†’
196â†’        /**
197â†’         * An existing [DateTimeFormat] for the time part.
198â†’         *
199â†’         * Example:
200â†’         * ```
201â†’         * time(LocalTime.Formats.ISO)
202â†’         * ```
203â†’         */
204â†’        public fun time(format: DateTimeFormat<LocalTime>)
205â†’    }
206â†’
207â†’    /**
208â†’     * Functions specific to the date-time format builders containing the local-date and local-time fields.
209â†’     */
210â†’    public sealed interface WithDateTime : WithDate, WithTime {
211â†’        /**
212â†’         * An existing [DateTimeFormat] for the date-time part.
213â†’         *
214â†’         * Example:
215â†’         * ```
216â†’         * dateTime(LocalDateTime.Formats.ISO)
217â†’         * ```
218â†’         */
219â†’        public fun dateTime(format: DateTimeFormat<LocalDateTime>)
220â†’    }
221â†’
222â†’    /**
223â†’     * Functions specific to the date-time format builders containing the UTC-offset fields.
224â†’     */
225â†’    public sealed interface WithUtcOffset : DateTimeFormatBuilder {
226â†’        /**
227â†’         * The total number of hours in the UTC offset, including the sign.
228â†’         *
229â†’         * By default, it's zero-padded to two digits, but this can be changed with [padding].
230â†’         *
231â†’         * This field has the default value of 0. If you want to omit it, use [optional].
232â†’         */
233â†’        public fun offsetHours(padding: Padding = Padding.ZERO)
234â†’
235â†’        /**
236â†’         * The minute-of-hour of the UTC offset.
237â†’         *
238â†’         * By default, it's zero-padded to two digits, but this can be changed with [padding].
239â†’         *
240â†’         * This field has the default value of 0. If you want to omit it, use [optional].
241â†’         */
242â†’        public fun offsetMinutesOfHour(padding: Padding = Padding.ZERO)
243â†’
244â†’        /**
245â†’         * The second-of-minute of the UTC offset.
246â†’         *
247â†’         * By default, it's zero-padded to two digits, but this can be changed with [padding].
248â†’         *
249â†’         * This field has the default value of 0. If you want to omit it, use [optional].
250â†’         */
251â†’        public fun offsetSecondsOfMinute(padding: Padding = Padding.ZERO)
252â†’
253â†’        /**
254â†’         * An existing [DateTimeFormat] for the UTC offset part.
255â†’         *
256â†’         * Example:
257â†’         * ```
258â†’         * offset(UtcOffset.Formats.FOUR_DIGITS)
259â†’         * ```
260â†’         */
261â†’        public fun offset(format: DateTimeFormat<UtcOffset>)
262â†’    }
263â†’
264â†’    /**
265â†’     * Builder for formats for values that have all the date-time components:
266â†’     * date, time, UTC offset, and the timezone ID.
267â†’     */
268â†’    public sealed interface WithDateTimeComponents : WithDateTime, WithUtcOffset {
269â†’        /**
270â†’         * The IANA time zone identifier, for example, "Europe/Berlin".
271â†’         *
272â†’         * When formatting, the timezone identifier is supplied as is, without any validation.
273â†’         * On parsing, [TimeZone.availableZoneIds] is used to validate the identifier.
274â†’         */
275â†’        public fun timeZoneId()
276â†’
277â†’        /**
278â†’         * An existing [DateTimeFormat].
279â†’         *
280â†’         * Example:
281â†’         * ```
282â†’         * dateTimeComponents(DateTimeComponents.Formats.RFC_1123)
283â†’         * ```
284â†’         */
285â†’        public fun dateTimeComponents(format: DateTimeFormat<DateTimeComponents>)
286â†’    }
287â†’}
288â†’
289â†’/**
290â†’ * The fractional part of the second without the leading dot.
291â†’ *
292â†’ * When formatting, the decimal fraction will round the number to fit in the specified [maxLength] and will add
293â†’ * trailing zeroes to the specified [minLength].
294â†’ *
295â†’ * Additionally, [grouping] is a list, where the i'th (1-based) element specifies how many trailing zeros to add during
296â†’ * formatting when the number would have i digits.
297â†’ *
298â†’ * When parsing, the parser will require that the fraction is at least [minLength] and at most [maxLength]
299â†’ * digits long.
300â†’ *
301â†’ * This field has the default value of 0. If you want to omit it, use [optional].
302â†’ *
303â†’ * @throws IllegalArgumentException if [minLength] is greater than [maxLength] or if either is not in the range 1..9.
304â†’ */
305â†’internal fun DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime.secondFractionInternal(
306â†’    minLength: Int,
307â†’    maxLength: Int,
308â†’    grouping: List<Int>
309â†’) {
310â†’    @Suppress("NO_ELSE_IN_WHEN")
311â†’    when (this) {
312â†’        is AbstractWithTimeBuilder -> addFormatStructureForTime(
313â†’            BasicFormatStructure(FractionalSecondDirective(minLength, maxLength, grouping))
314â†’        )
315â†’    }
316â†’}
317â†’
318â†’/**
319â†’ * A format along with other ways to parse the same portion of the value.
320â†’ *
321â†’ * When parsing, first, [primaryFormat] is used; if parsing the whole string fails using that, the formats
322â†’ * from [alternativeFormats] are tried in order.
323â†’ *
324â†’ * When formatting, the [primaryFormat] is used to format the value, and [alternativeFormats] are ignored.
325â†’ *
326â†’ * Example:
327â†’ * ```
328â†’ * alternativeParsing(
329â†’ *   { dayOfMonth(); char('-'); monthNumber() },
330â†’ *   { monthNumber(); char(' '); dayOfMonth() },
331â†’ * ) { monthNumber(); char('/'); dayOfMonth() }
332â†’ * ```
333â†’ *
334â†’ * This will always format a date as `MM/DD`, but will also accept `DD-MM` and `MM DD`.
335â†’ */
336â†’@Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")
337â†’public fun <T : DateTimeFormatBuilder> T.alternativeParsing(
338â†’    vararg alternativeFormats: T.() -> Unit,
339â†’    primaryFormat: T.() -> Unit
340â†’): Unit = when (this) {
341â†’    is AbstractDateTimeFormatBuilder<*, *> ->
342â†’        appendAlternativeParsingImpl(
343â†’            *alternativeFormats as Array<out AbstractDateTimeFormatBuilder<*, *>.() -> Unit>,
344â†’            mainFormat = primaryFormat as (AbstractDateTimeFormatBuilder<*, *>.() -> Unit)
345â†’        )
346â†’
347â†’    else -> throw IllegalStateException("impossible")
348â†’}
349â†’
350â†’/**
351â†’ * An optional section.
352â†’ *
353â†’ * When formatting, the section is formatted if the value of any field in the block is not equal to the default value.
354â†’ * Only [optional] calls where all the fields have default values are permitted.
355â†’ *
356â†’ * Example:
357â†’ * ```
358â†’ * offsetHours(); char(':'); offsetMinutesOfHour()
359â†’ * optional { char(':'); offsetSecondsOfMinute() }
360â†’ * ```
361â†’ *
362â†’ * Here, because seconds have the default value of zero, they are formatted only if they are not equal to zero, so the
363â†’ * UTC offset `+18:30:00` gets formatted as `"+18:30"`, but `+18:30:01` becomes `"+18:30:01"`.
364â†’ *
365â†’ * When parsing, either [format] or, if that fails, the literal [ifZero] are parsed. If the [ifZero] string is parsed,
366â†’ * the values in [format] get assigned their default values.
367â†’ *
368â†’ * [ifZero] defines the string that is used if values are the default ones.
369â†’ *
370â†’ * @throws IllegalArgumentException if not all fields used in [format] have a default value.
371â†’ */
372â†’@Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")
373â†’public fun <T : DateTimeFormatBuilder> T.optional(ifZero: String = "", format: T.() -> Unit): Unit = when (this) {
374â†’    is AbstractDateTimeFormatBuilder<*, *> -> appendOptionalImpl(
375â†’        onZero = ifZero,
376â†’        format as (AbstractDateTimeFormatBuilder<*, *>.() -> Unit)
377â†’    )
378â†’
379â†’    else -> throw IllegalStateException("impossible")
380â†’}
381â†’
382â†’/**
383â†’ * A literal character.
384â†’ *
385â†’ * This is a shorthand for `chars(value.toString())`.
386â†’ */
387â†’public fun DateTimeFormatBuilder.char(value: Char): Unit = chars(value.toString())
388â†’
389â†’internal interface AbstractDateTimeFormatBuilder<Target, ActualSelf> :
390â†’    DateTimeFormatBuilder where ActualSelf : AbstractDateTimeFormatBuilder<Target, ActualSelf> {
391â†’
392â†’    val actualBuilder: AppendableFormatStructure<Target>
393â†’    fun createEmpty(): ActualSelf
394â†’
395â†’    fun appendAlternativeParsingImpl(
396â†’        vararg otherFormats: ActualSelf.() -> Unit,
397â†’        mainFormat: ActualSelf.() -> Unit
398â†’    ) {
399â†’        val others = otherFormats.map { block ->
400â†’            createEmpty().also { block(it) }.actualBuilder.build()
401â†’        }
402â†’        val main = createEmpty().also { mainFormat(it) }.actualBuilder.build()
403â†’        actualBuilder.add(AlternativesParsingFormatStructure(main, others))
404â†’    }
405â†’
406â†’    fun appendOptionalImpl(
407â†’        onZero: String,
408â†’        format: ActualSelf.() -> Unit
409â†’    ) {
410â†’        actualBuilder.add(OptionalFormatStructure(onZero, createEmpty().also { format(it) }.actualBuilder.build()))
411â†’    }
412â†’
413â†’    override fun chars(value: String) = actualBuilder.add(ConstantFormatStructure(value))
414â†’
415â†’    fun build(): CachedFormatStructure<Target> = CachedFormatStructure(actualBuilder.build().formats)
416â†’}
417â†’
418â†’internal fun <T> FormatStructure<T>.builderString(constants: List<Pair<String, CachedFormatStructure<*>>>): String =
419â†’    when (this) {
420â†’        is BasicFormatStructure -> directive.builderRepresentation
421â†’        is ConstantFormatStructure -> if (string.length == 1) {
422â†’            "${DateTimeFormatBuilder::char.name}(${string[0].toKotlinCode()})"
423â†’        } else {
424â†’            "${DateTimeFormatBuilder::chars.name}(${string.toKotlinCode()})"
425â†’        }
426â†’
427â†’        is SignedFormatStructure -> {
428â†’            if (format is BasicFormatStructure && format.directive is UtcOffsetWholeHoursDirective) {
429â†’                format.directive.builderRepresentation
430â†’            } else {
431â†’                buildString {
432â†’                    if (withPlusSign) appendLine("withSharedSign(outputPlus = true) {")
433â†’                    else appendLine("withSharedSign {")
434â†’                    appendLine(format.builderString(constants).prependIndent(CODE_INDENT))
435â†’                    append("}")
436â†’                }
437â†’            }
438â†’        }
439â†’
440â†’        is OptionalFormatStructure -> buildString {
441â†’            if (onZero == "") {
442â†’                appendLine("${DateTimeFormatBuilder::optional.name} {")
443â†’            } else {
444â†’                appendLine("${DateTimeFormatBuilder::optional.name}(${onZero.toKotlinCode()}) {")
445â†’            }
446â†’            val subformat = format.builderString(constants)
447â†’            if (subformat.isNotEmpty()) {
448â†’                appendLine(subformat.prependIndent(CODE_INDENT))
449â†’            }
450â†’            append("}")
451â†’        }
452â†’
453â†’        is AlternativesParsingFormatStructure -> buildString {
454â†’            append("${DateTimeFormatBuilder::alternativeParsing.name}(")
455â†’            for (alternative in formats) {
456â†’                appendLine("{")
457â†’                val subformat = alternative.builderString(constants)
458â†’                if (subformat.isNotEmpty()) {
459â†’                    appendLine(subformat.prependIndent(CODE_INDENT))
460â†’                }
461â†’                append("}, ")
462â†’            }
463â†’            if (this[length - 2] == ',') {
464â†’                repeat(2) {
465â†’                    deleteAt(length - 1)
466â†’                }
467â†’            }
468â†’            appendLine(") {")
469â†’            appendLine(mainFormat.builderString(constants).prependIndent(CODE_INDENT))
470â†’            append("}")
471â†’        }
472â†’
473â†’        is ConcatenatedFormatStructure -> buildString {
474â†’            if (formats.isNotEmpty()) {
475â†’                var index = 0
476â†’                loop@ while (index < formats.size) {
477â†’                    searchConstant@ for (constant in constants) {
478â†’                        val constantDirectives = constant.second.formats
479â†’                        if (formats.size - index >= constantDirectives.size) {
480â†’                            for (i in constantDirectives.indices) {
481â†’                                if (formats[index + i] != constantDirectives[i]) {
482â†’                                    continue@searchConstant
483â†’                                }
484â†’                            }
485â†’                            append(constant.first)
486â†’                            index += constantDirectives.size
487â†’                            if (index < formats.size) {
488â†’                                appendLine()
489â†’                            }
490â†’                            continue@loop
491â†’                        }
492â†’                    }
493â†’                    if (index == formats.size - 1) {
494â†’                        append(formats.last().builderString(constants))
495â†’                    } else {
496â†’                        appendLine(formats[index].builderString(constants))
497â†’                    }
498â†’                    ++index
499â†’                }
500â†’            }
501â†’        }
502â†’    }
503â†’
504â†’private const val CODE_INDENT = "    "
505â†’

function.name: edit
function.arguments:
{"new_string":"/**
 * Common functions for all format builders.
 *
 * This builder is used to define custom date-time formats using a DSL.
 * It is used as a receiver in the `Format { ... }` blocks of various date-time classes.
 *
 * Example:
 * ```
 * val format = LocalDate.Format {
 *     year()
 *     char('-')
 *     monthNumber()
 *     char('-')
 *     dayOfMonth()
 * }
 * ```
 */
public sealed interface DateTimeFormatBuilder {","file_path":"core/common/src/format/DateTimeFormatBuilder.kt","old_string":"/**
 * Common functions for all format builders.
 */
public sealed interface DateTimeFormatBuilder {"}

 13â†’/**

 21â†’     */

 23â†’) {
 24â†’    init {

 26â†’        names.indices.forEach { ix ->
 27â†’            require(names[ix].isNotEmpty()) { "A month name can not be empty" }
 28â†’            for (ix2 in 0 until ix) {
 29â†’                require(names[ix] != names[ix2]) {

 31â†’                }
 32â†’            }
 33â†’        }
 34â†’    }

138â†’internal fun <T> requireParsedField(field: T?, name: String): T {
139â†’    if (field == null) {
140â†’        throw DateTimeFormatException("Can not create a $name from the given input: the field $name is missing")
141â†’    }
142â†’    return field

145â†’internal interface DateFieldContainer {
146â†’    var year: Int?
147â†’    var monthNumber: Int?
148â†’    var dayOfMonth: Int?
149â†’    var isoDayOfWeek: Int?
150â†’}
151â†’
152â†’private object DateFields {
153â†’    val year = GenericFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(DateFieldContainer::year))
154â†’    val month = UnsignedFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(DateFieldContainer::monthNumber), minValue = 1, maxValue = 12)
155â†’    val dayOfMonth = UnsignedFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(DateFieldContainer::dayOfMonth), minValue = 1, maxValue = 31)
156â†’    val isoDayOfWeek = UnsignedFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(DateFieldContainer::isoDayOfWeek), minValue = 1, maxValue = 7)
157â†’}

159â†’/**
160â†’ * A [kotlinx.datetime.LocalDate], but potentially incomplete and inconsistent.
161â†’ */
162â†’internal class IncompleteLocalDate(
163â†’    override var year: Int? = null,
164â†’    override var monthNumber: Int? = null,
165â†’    override var dayOfMonth: Int? = null,
166â†’    override var isoDayOfWeek: Int? = null
167â†’) : DateFieldContainer, Copyable<IncompleteLocalDate> {
168â†’    fun toLocalDate(): LocalDate {
169â†’        val date = LocalDate(
170â†’            requireParsedField(year, "year"),
171â†’            requireParsedField(monthNumber, "monthNumber"),
172â†’            requireParsedField(dayOfMonth, "dayOfMonth")
173â†’        )
174â†’        isoDayOfWeek?.let {
175â†’            if (it != date.dayOfWeek.isoDayNumber) {
176â†’                throw DateTimeFormatException(
177â†’                    "Can not create a LocalDate from the given input: " +
178â†’                        "the day of week is ${DayOfWeek(it)} but the date is $date, which is a ${date.dayOfWeek}"
179â†’                )
180â†’            }
181â†’        }
182â†’        return date
183â†’    }
184â†’
185â†’    fun populateFrom(date: LocalDate) {
186â†’        year = date.year
187â†’        monthNumber = date.monthNumber
188â†’        dayOfMonth = date.dayOfMonth
189â†’        isoDayOfWeek = date.dayOfWeek.isoDayNumber
190â†’    }
191â†’
192â†’    override fun copy(): IncompleteLocalDate = IncompleteLocalDate(year, monthNumber, dayOfMonth, isoDayOfWeek)
193â†’
194â†’    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
195â†’        other is IncompleteLocalDate && year == other.year && monthNumber == other.monthNumber &&
196â†’            dayOfMonth == other.dayOfMonth && isoDayOfWeek == other.isoDayOfWeek
197â†’
198â†’    override fun hashCode(): Int =
199â†’        year.hashCode() * 31 + monthNumber.hashCode() * 31 + dayOfMonth.hashCode() * 31 + isoDayOfWeek.hashCode() * 31
200â†’
201â†’    override fun toString(): String =
202â†’        "${year ?: "??"}-${monthNumber ?: "??"}-${dayOfMonth ?: "??"} (day of week is ${isoDayOfWeek ?: "??"})"
203â†’}
204â†’
205â†’private class YearDirective(private val padding: Padding, private val isYearOfEra: Boolean = false) :
206â†’    SignedIntFieldFormatDirective<DateFieldContainer>(
207â†’        DateFields.year,
208â†’        minDigits = padding.minDigits(4),
209â†’        maxDigits = null,
210â†’        spacePadding = padding.spaces(4),
211â†’        outputPlusOnExceededWidth = 4,
212â†’    ) {
213â†’    override val builderRepresentation: String
214â†’        get() = when (padding) {
215â†’            Padding.ZERO -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::year.name}()"
216â†’            else -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::year.name}(${padding.toKotlinCode()})"
217â†’        }.let {
218â†’            if (isYearOfEra) {
219â†’                it + YEAR_OF_ERA_COMMENT
220â†’            } else it
221â†’        }
222â†’
223â†’    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
224â†’        other is YearDirective && padding == other.padding && isYearOfEra == other.isYearOfEra
225â†’
226â†’    override fun hashCode(): Int = padding.hashCode() * 31 + isYearOfEra.hashCode()
227â†’}

229â†’private class ReducedYearDirective(val base: Int, private val isYearOfEra: Boolean = false) :
230â†’    ReducedIntFieldDirective<DateFieldContainer>(
231â†’        DateFields.year,
232â†’        digits = 2,
233â†’        base = base,
234â†’    ) {
235â†’    override val builderRepresentation: String
236â†’        get() =
237â†’            "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::yearTwoDigits.name}($base)".let {
238â†’                if (isYearOfEra) {
239â†’                    it + YEAR_OF_ERA_COMMENT
240â†’                } else it
241â†’            }
242â†’
243â†’    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
244â†’        other is ReducedYearDirective && base == other.base && isYearOfEra == other.isYearOfEra
245â†’
246â†’    override fun hashCode(): Int = base.hashCode() * 31 + isYearOfEra.hashCode()
247â†’}

249â†’private const val YEAR_OF_ERA_COMMENT =
250â†’    " /** TODO: the original format had an `y` directive, so the behavior is different on years earlier than 1 AD. See the [kotlinx.datetime.format.byUnicodePattern] documentation for details. */"
251â†’
252â†’/**
253â†’ * A special directive for year-of-era that behaves equivalently to [DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.year].
254â†’ * This is the result of calling [byUnicodePattern] on a pattern that uses the ubiquitous "y" symbol.
255â†’ * We need a separate directive so that, when using [DateTimeFormat.formatAsKotlinBuilderDsl], we can print an
256â†’ * additional comment and explain that the behavior was not preserved exactly.
257â†’ */
258â†’internal fun DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.yearOfEra(padding: Padding) {
259â†’    @Suppress("NO_ELSE_IN_WHEN")
260â†’    when (this) {
261â†’        is AbstractWithDateBuilder -> addFormatStructureForDate(
262â†’            BasicFormatStructure(YearDirective(padding, isYearOfEra = true))
263â†’        )
264â†’    }
265â†’}
266â†’
267â†’/**
268â†’ * A special directive for year-of-era that behaves equivalently to [DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.year].
269â†’ * This is the result of calling [byUnicodePattern] on a pattern that uses the ubiquitous "y" symbol.
270â†’ * We need a separate directive so that, when using [DateTimeFormat.formatAsKotlinBuilderDsl], we can print an
271â†’ * additional comment and explain that the behavior was not preserved exactly.
272â†’ */
273â†’internal fun DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.yearOfEraTwoDigits(baseYear: Int) {

275â†’    when (this) {

279â†’    }
280â†’}

283â†’    UnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<DateFieldContainer>(

308â†’private class DayDirective(private val padding: Padding) :

324â†’private class DayOfWeekDirective(private val names: DayOfWeekNames) :
325â†’    NamedUnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<DateFieldContainer>(DateFields.isoDayOfWeek, names.names, "dayOfWeekName") {
326â†’
327â†’    override val builderRepresentation: String

329â†’            "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::dayOfWeek.name}(${names.toKotlinCode()})"
330â†’
331â†’    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean = other is DayOfWeekDirective && names.names == other.names.names
332â†’    override fun hashCode(): Int = names.names.hashCode()
333â†’}
334â†’
335â†’internal class LocalDateFormat(override val actualFormat: CachedFormatStructure<DateFieldContainer>) :
336â†’    AbstractDateTimeFormat<LocalDate, IncompleteLocalDate>() {
337â†’    override fun intermediateFromValue(value: LocalDate): IncompleteLocalDate =
338â†’        IncompleteLocalDate().apply { populateFrom(value) }
339â†’
340â†’    override fun valueFromIntermediate(intermediate: IncompleteLocalDate): LocalDate = intermediate.toLocalDate()
341â†’
342â†’    override val emptyIntermediate get() = emptyIncompleteLocalDate
343â†’
344â†’    companion object {
345â†’        fun build(block: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.() -> Unit): DateTimeFormat<LocalDate> {
346â†’            val builder = Builder(AppendableFormatStructure())
347â†’            builder.block()
348â†’            return LocalDateFormat(builder.build())
349â†’        }
350â†’    }
351â†’
352â†’    internal class Builder(override val actualBuilder: AppendableFormatStructure<DateFieldContainer>) :
353â†’        AbstractDateTimeFormatBuilder<DateFieldContainer, Builder>, AbstractWithDateBuilder {
354â†’
355â†’        override fun addFormatStructureForDate(structure: FormatStructure<DateFieldContainer>) =
356â†’            actualBuilder.add(structure)
357â†’
358â†’        override fun createEmpty(): Builder = Builder(AppendableFormatStructure())
359â†’    }
360â†’}
361â†’
362â†’internal interface AbstractWithDateBuilder : DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate {
363â†’    fun addFormatStructureForDate(structure: FormatStructure<DateFieldContainer>)
364â†’
365â†’    override fun year(padding: Padding) =
366â†’        addFormatStructureForDate(BasicFormatStructure(YearDirective(padding)))

368â†’    override fun yearTwoDigits(baseYear: Int) =

  8â†’import kotlinx.datetime.LocalTime

 10â†’import kotlinx.datetime.internal.DecimalFraction
 11â†’import kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.*
 12â†’import kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.parser.Copyable
 13â†’
 14â†’/**
 15â†’ * The AM/PM marker that indicates whether the hour in range `1..12` is before or after noon.
 16â†’ */
 17â†’public enum class AmPmMarker {
 18â†’    /** The time is before noon. */
 19â†’    AM,
 20â†’
 21â†’    /** The time is after noon. */
 22â†’    PM,
 23â†’}
 24â†’
 25â†’internal interface TimeFieldContainer {
 26â†’    var minute: Int?
 27â†’    var second: Int?
 28â†’    var nanosecond: Int?
 29â†’    var hour: Int?
 30â†’    var hourOfAmPm: Int?
 31â†’    var amPm: AmPmMarker?
 32â†’
 33â†’    var fractionOfSecond: DecimalFraction?
 34â†’        get() = nanosecond?.let { DecimalFraction(it, 9) }
 35â†’        set(value) {
 36â†’            nanosecond = value?.fractionalPartWithNDigits(9)
 37â†’        }
 38â†’}
 39â†’
 40â†’private object TimeFields {
 41â†’    val hour = UnsignedFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(TimeFieldContainer::hour), minValue = 0, maxValue = 23)
 42â†’    val minute = UnsignedFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(TimeFieldContainer::minute), minValue = 0, maxValue = 59)
 43â†’    val second =
 44â†’        UnsignedFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(TimeFieldContainer::second), minValue = 0, maxValue = 59, defaultValue = 0)
 45â†’    val fractionOfSecond =
 46â†’        GenericFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(TimeFieldContainer::fractionOfSecond), defaultValue = DecimalFraction(0, 9))
 47â†’    val amPm = GenericFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(TimeFieldContainer::amPm))
 48â†’    val hourOfAmPm = UnsignedFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(TimeFieldContainer::hourOfAmPm), minValue = 1, maxValue = 12)
 49â†’}
 50â†’
 51â†’internal class IncompleteLocalTime(
 52â†’    override var hour: Int? = null,
 53â†’    override var hourOfAmPm: Int? = null,
 54â†’    override var amPm: AmPmMarker? = null,
 55â†’    override var minute: Int? = null,
 56â†’    override var second: Int? = null,
 57â†’    override var nanosecond: Int? = null
 58â†’) : TimeFieldContainer, Copyable<IncompleteLocalTime> {
 59â†’    fun toLocalTime(): LocalTime {
 60â†’        val hour: Int = hour?.let { hour ->
 61â†’            hourOfAmPm?.let {
 62â†’                require((hour + 11) % 12 + 1 == it) { "Inconsistent hour and hour-of-am-pm: hour is $hour, but hour-of-am-pm is $it" }
 63â†’            }
 64â†’            amPm?.let { amPm ->
 65â†’                require((amPm == AmPmMarker.PM) == (hour >= 12)) {
 66â†’                    "Inconsistent hour and the AM/PM marker: hour is $hour, but the AM/PM marker is $amPm"
 67â†’                }
 68â†’            }
 69â†’            hour
 70â†’        } ?: hourOfAmPm?.let { hourOfAmPm ->
 71â†’            amPm?.let { amPm ->
 72â†’                hourOfAmPm.let { if (it == 12) 0 else it } + if (amPm == AmPmMarker.PM) 12 else 0
 73â†’            }
 74â†’        } ?: throw DateTimeFormatException("Incomplete time: missing hour")
 75â†’        return LocalTime(
 76â†’            hour,
 77â†’            requireParsedField(minute, "minute"),
 78â†’            second ?: 0,
 79â†’            nanosecond ?: 0,
 80â†’        )
 81â†’    }
 82â†’
 83â†’    fun populateFrom(localTime: LocalTime) {
 84â†’        hour = localTime.hour
 85â†’        hourOfAmPm = (localTime.hour + 11) % 12 + 1
 86â†’        amPm = if (localTime.hour >= 12) AmPmMarker.PM else AmPmMarker.AM
 87â†’        minute = localTime.minute
 88â†’        second = localTime.second
 89â†’        nanosecond = localTime.nanosecond
 90â†’    }
 91â†’
 92â†’    override fun copy(): IncompleteLocalTime = IncompleteLocalTime(hour, hourOfAmPm, amPm, minute, second, nanosecond)
 93â†’
 94â†’    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
 95â†’        other is IncompleteLocalTime && hour == other.hour && hourOfAmPm == other.hourOfAmPm && amPm == other.amPm &&
 96â†’            minute == other.minute && second == other.second && nanosecond == other.nanosecond
 97â†’
 98â†’    override fun hashCode(): Int =
 99â†’        (hour ?: 0) * 31 + (hourOfAmPm ?: 0) * 31 + (amPm?.hashCode() ?: 0) * 31 + (minute ?: 0) * 31 +
100â†’            (second ?: 0) * 31 + (nanosecond ?: 0)
101â†’
102â†’    override fun toString(): String =
103â†’        "${hour ?: "??"}:${minute ?: "??"}:${second ?: "??"}.${
104â†’            nanosecond?.let { nanos ->
105â†’                nanos.toString().let { it.padStart(9 - it.length, '0') }
106â†’            } ?: "???"
107â†’        }"
108â†’}

110â†’internal interface AbstractWithTimeBuilder : DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime {
111â†’    fun addFormatStructureForTime(structure: FormatStructure<TimeFieldContainer>)
112â†’
113â†’    override fun hour(padding: Padding) = addFormatStructureForTime(BasicFormatStructure(HourDirective(padding)))
114â†’    override fun amPmHour(padding: Padding) =
115â†’        addFormatStructureForTime(BasicFormatStructure(AmPmHourDirective(padding)))
116â†’

118â†’        addFormatStructureForTime(BasicFormatStructure(AmPmMarkerDirective(am, pm)))
119â†’

122â†’    override fun secondFraction(minLength: Int, maxLength: Int) =
123â†’        addFormatStructureForTime(BasicFormatStructure(FractionalSecondDirective(minLength, maxLength)))
124â†’
125â†’    @Suppress("NO_ELSE_IN_WHEN")
126â†’    override fun time(format: DateTimeFormat<LocalTime>) = when (format) {
127â†’        is LocalTimeFormat -> addFormatStructureForTime(format.actualFormat)
128â†’    }
129â†’}
130â†’
131â†’private class HourDirective(private val padding: Padding) :
132â†’    UnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<TimeFieldContainer>(
133â†’        TimeFields.hour,
134â†’        minDigits = padding.minDigits(2),
135â†’        spacePadding = padding.spaces(2)
136â†’    ) {
137â†’    override val builderRepresentation: String
138â†’        get() = when (padding) {
139â†’            Padding.ZERO -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime::hour.name}()"
140â†’            else -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime::hour.name}(${padding.toKotlinCode()})"
141â†’        }
142â†’
143â†’    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean = other is HourDirective && padding == other.padding
144â†’    override fun hashCode(): Int = padding.hashCode()
145â†’}
146â†’
147â†’private class AmPmHourDirective(private val padding: Padding) :
148â†’    UnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<TimeFieldContainer>(
149â†’        TimeFields.hourOfAmPm, minDigits = padding.minDigits(2),
150â†’        spacePadding = padding.spaces(2)
151â†’    ) {
152â†’    override val builderRepresentation: String
153â†’        get() = when (padding) {
154â†’            Padding.ZERO -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime::amPmHour.name}()"
155â†’            else -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime::amPmHour.name}(${padding.toKotlinCode()})"
156â†’        }
157â†’
158â†’    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean = other is AmPmHourDirective && padding == other.padding
159â†’    override fun hashCode(): Int = padding.hashCode()
160â†’}
161â†’
162â†’private class AmPmMarkerDirective(private val amString: String, private val pmString: String) :
163â†’    NamedEnumIntFieldFormatDirective<TimeFieldContainer, AmPmMarker>(
164â†’        TimeFields.amPm, mapOf(
165â†’            AmPmMarker.AM to amString,
166â†’            AmPmMarker.PM to pmString,
167â†’        ),
168â†’        "AM/PM marker"
169â†’    ) {
170â†’
171â†’    override val builderRepresentation: String
172â†’        get() =
173â†’            "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime::amPmMarker.name}($amString, $pmString)"
174â†’
175â†’    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
176â†’        other is AmPmMarkerDirective && amString == other.amString && pmString == other.pmString
177â†’
178â†’    override fun hashCode(): Int = 31 * amString.hashCode() + pmString.hashCode()
179â†’}
180â†’
181â†’private class MinuteDirective(private val padding: Padding) :
182â†’    UnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<TimeFieldContainer>(
183â†’        TimeFields.minute,
184â†’        minDigits = padding.minDigits(2),
185â†’        spacePadding = padding.spaces(2)
186â†’    ) {
187â†’
188â†’    override val builderRepresentation: String
189â†’        get() = when (padding) {
190â†’            Padding.ZERO -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime::minute.name}()"
191â†’            else -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime::minute.name}(${padding.toKotlinCode()})"
192â†’        }
193â†’
194â†’    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean = other is MinuteDirective && padding == other.padding
195â†’    override fun hashCode(): Int = padding.hashCode()
196â†’}
197â†’
198â†’private class SecondDirective(private val padding: Padding) :
199â†’    UnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<TimeFieldContainer>(
200â†’        TimeFields.second,
201â†’        minDigits = padding.minDigits(2),
202â†’        spacePadding = padding.spaces(2)
203â†’    ) {
204â†’
205â†’    override val builderRepresentation: String
206â†’        get() = when (padding) {
207â†’            Padding.ZERO -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime::second.name}()"
208â†’            else -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime::second.name}(${padding.toKotlinCode()})"
209â†’        }
210â†’
211â†’    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean = other is SecondDirective && padding == other.padding
212â†’    override fun hashCode(): Int = padding.hashCode()
213â†’}
214â†’
215â†’internal class FractionalSecondDirective(
216â†’    private val minDigits: Int,
217â†’    private val maxDigits: Int,
218â†’    zerosToAdd: List<Int> = NO_EXTRA_ZEROS,
219â†’) :
220â†’    DecimalFractionFieldFormatDirective<TimeFieldContainer>(
221â†’        TimeFields.fractionOfSecond,
222â†’        minDigits,
223â†’        maxDigits,
224â†’        zerosToAdd
225â†’    ) {
226â†’
227â†’    override val builderRepresentation: String
228â†’        get() {
229â†’            val ref = "secondFraction" // can't directly reference `secondFraction` due to resolution ambiguity
230â†’            // we ignore `grouping`, as it's not representable in the end users' code
231â†’            return when {
232â†’                minDigits == 1 && maxDigits == 9 -> "$ref()"
233â†’                minDigits == 1 -> "$ref(maxLength = $maxDigits)"
234â†’                maxDigits == 1 -> "$ref(minLength = $minDigits)"
235â†’                maxDigits == minDigits -> "$ref($minDigits)"
236â†’                else -> "$ref($minDigits, $maxDigits)"
237â†’            }
238â†’        }
239â†’
240â†’    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
241â†’        other is FractionalSecondDirective && minDigits == other.minDigits && maxDigits == other.maxDigits
242â†’
243â†’    override fun hashCode(): Int = 31 * minDigits + maxDigits
244â†’
245â†’    companion object {
246â†’        val NO_EXTRA_ZEROS = listOf(0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0)
247â†’        val GROUP_BY_THREE = listOf(2, 1, 0, 2, 1, 0, 2, 1, 0)
248â†’    }
249â†’}
250â†’
251â†’internal class LocalTimeFormat(override val actualFormat: CachedFormatStructure<TimeFieldContainer>) :
252â†’    AbstractDateTimeFormat<LocalTime, IncompleteLocalTime>() {
253â†’    override fun intermediateFromValue(value: LocalTime): IncompleteLocalTime =
254â†’        IncompleteLocalTime().apply { populateFrom(value) }
255â†’
256â†’    override fun valueFromIntermediate(intermediate: IncompleteLocalTime): LocalTime = intermediate.toLocalTime()
257â†’
258â†’    override val emptyIntermediate: IncompleteLocalTime get() = emptyIncompleteLocalTime
259â†’
260â†’    companion object {
261â†’        fun build(block: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime.() -> Unit): LocalTimeFormat {
262â†’            val builder = Builder(AppendableFormatStructure())
263â†’            builder.block()
264â†’            return LocalTimeFormat(builder.build())
265â†’        }
266â†’
267â†’    }
268â†’
269â†’    private class Builder(override val actualBuilder: AppendableFormatStructure<TimeFieldContainer>) :
270â†’        AbstractDateTimeFormatBuilder<TimeFieldContainer, Builder>, AbstractWithTimeBuilder {
271â†’
272â†’        override fun addFormatStructureForTime(structure: FormatStructure<TimeFieldContainer>) {
273â†’            actualBuilder.add(structure)
274â†’        }
275â†’
276â†’        override fun createEmpty(): Builder = Builder(AppendableFormatStructure())
277â†’    }
278â†’
279â†’}
280â†’
281â†’// these are constants so that the formats are not recreated every time they are used
282â†’internal val ISO_TIME by lazy {
283â†’    LocalTimeFormat.build {
284â†’        hour()
285â†’        char(':')
286â†’        minute()
287â†’        alternativeParsing({
288â†’            // intentionally empty
289â†’        }) {
290â†’            char(':')
291â†’            second()
292â†’            optional {
293â†’                char('.')
294â†’                secondFraction(1, 9)
295â†’            }
296â†’        }
297â†’    }
298â†’}
299â†’
300â†’private val emptyIncompleteLocalTime = IncompleteLocalTime()
301â†’

content.text:
The API reference for the `kotlinx-datetime` library was improved by systematically updating the KDoc documentation for all major entities. The enhancements focus on providing extensive information about what each entity represents, its ISO-8601 compliance, how it can be acquired, its time scale, and practical usage examples.

Key improvements include:
- **Core Entities**: Added detailed class-level documentation for `Instant`, `Clock`, `LocalDate`, `LocalDateTime`, `LocalTime`, `TimeZone`, `UtcOffset`, `Month`, and `DayOfWeek`. These now include "How to acquire" sections and basic usage examples.
- **ISO-8601 Compliance**: Explicitly documented the ISO-8601 compliance for date and time types, including the use of the UTC-SLS time scale for `Instant`.
- **Interoperability**: Added information about JVM interoperability, mentioning conversion functions like `toJava...()` and `toKotlin...()` for types that have a corresponding `java.time` equivalent.
- **Arithmetic and Gotchas**: Documented common pitfalls, such as the difference between physical time arithmetic on `Instant` versus calendar-aware arithmetic.
- **Formatting API**: Improved the documentation for the formatting DSL, including `DateTimeFormat`, `DateTimeFormatBuilder`, `DateTimeComponents`, and supporting types like `MonthNames` and `DayOfWeekNames`.
- **Consistency**: Ensured that the documentation is consistent with the library's `README` and that key concepts explained there are also discoverable in the API reference.
- **Exceptions**: Enhanced the documentation for `DateTimeArithmeticException` and `IllegalTimeZoneException` to provide better context on when they are thrown.

These changes ensure a more thorough and helpful API reference that guides developers on the intended use of the library's various components.

### Summary of Changes

- **`DayOfWeek.kt` & `Month.kt`**: Added details on ISO-8601 compliance and acquisition methods.
- **`Clock.kt`**: Expanded on the contract of the `Clock` interface and the behavior of `Clock.System`.
- **`Instant.kt`**: Added a basic usage example, platform-specific range notes, and JVM conversion details.
- **`LocalDate.kt`, `LocalDateTime.kt`, `LocalTime.kt`**: Provided extensive descriptions of what they represent, how to acquire them, and usage recommendations.
- **`TimeZone.kt` & `UtcOffset.kt`**: Added details on acquisition and practical usage examples.
- **`DateTimePeriod.kt` & `DateTimeUnit.kt`**: Explained the difference between calendar-based periods and fixed-duration physical time.
- **`DateTimeComponents.kt`**: Detailed its role in complex parsing/formatting scenarios.
- **`DateTimeFormat.kt` & `DateTimeFormatBuilder.kt`**: Added usage examples for the formatting DSL.
- **`LocalDateFormat.kt` & `LocalTimeFormat.kt`**: Improved documentation for names and markers used in formatting.
- **`Exceptions.kt`**: Added context to date-time related exceptions.

The changes were made primarily in the common source set to ensure they are available across all platforms.

23â†’     */

29â†’

31â†’}
32â†’
33â†’/**
34â†’ * Returns the current date at the given [time zone][timeZone], according to [this Clock][this].

39â†’/**

47â†’@ExperimentalTime
48â†’private class InstantTimeMark(private val instant: Instant, private val clock: Clock) : ComparableTimeMark {
49â†’    override fun elapsedNow(): Duration = saturatingDiff(clock.now(), instant)
50â†’
51â†’    override fun plus(duration: Duration): ComparableTimeMark = InstantTimeMark(instant.saturatingAdd(duration), clock)
52â†’    override fun minus(duration: Duration): ComparableTimeMark = InstantTimeMark(instant.saturatingAdd(-duration), clock)
53â†’
54â†’    override fun minus(other: ComparableTimeMark): Duration {
55â†’        if (other !is InstantTimeMark || other.clock != this.clock) {
56â†’            throw IllegalArgumentException("Subtracting or comparing time marks from different time sources is not possible: $this and $other")
57â†’        }
58â†’        return saturatingDiff(this.instant, other.instant)
59â†’    }

66â†’

71â†’        if (isSaturated()) {

73â†’                throw IllegalArgumentException("Summing infinities of different signs")
74â†’            }
75â†’            return this
76â†’        }

80â†’        instant1 == instant2 ->
81â†’            Duration.ZERO
82â†’        instant1.isSaturated() || instant2.isSaturated() ->
83â†’            (instant1 - instant2) * Double.POSITIVE_INFINITY

 13â†’
 14â†’/**
 15â†’ * A moment in time.
 16â†’ *
 17â†’ * A point in time must be uniquely identified, so that it is independent of a time zone.
 18â†’ * For example, `1970-01-01, 00:00:00` does not represent a moment in time, since this would happen at different times
 19â†’ * in different time zones: someone in Tokyo would think its already `1970-01-01` several hours earlier than someone in
 20â†’ * Berlin would. To represent such entities, use [LocalDateTime].
 21â†’ * In contrast, "the moment the clocks in London first showed 00:00 on Jan 1, 2000" is a specific moment
 22â†’ * in time, as is "1970-01-01, 00:00:00 UTC+0", and so it can be represented as an [Instant].
 23â†’ *
 24â†’ * `Instant` uses the UTC-SLS (smeared leap second) time scale. This time scale doesn't contain instants
 25â†’ * corresponding to leap seconds, but instead "smears" positive and negative leap seconds among the last 1000 seconds
 26â†’ * of the day when a leap second happens.
 27â†’ *
 28â†’ * Some ways in which [Instant] can be acquired are:
 29â†’ * - [Clock.now] can be used to query the current moment for the given clock. With [Clock.System], it is the current

510â†’    other.until(this, unit)

512â†’/**
513â†’ * Formats this value using the given [format] using the given [offset].
514â†’ *
515â†’ * Equivalent to calling [DateTimeFormat.format] on [format] and using [DateTimeComponents.setDateTimeOffset] in
516â†’ * the lambda.
517â†’ *
518â†’ * [DateTimeComponents.Formats.ISO_DATE_TIME_OFFSET] is a format very similar to the one used by [toString].
519â†’ * The only difference is that [Instant.toString] adds trailing zeros to the fraction-of-second component so that the
520â†’ * number of digits after a dot is a multiple of three.
521â†’ */
522â†’public fun Instant.format(format: DateTimeFormat<DateTimeComponents>, offset: UtcOffset = UtcOffset.ZERO): String {
523â†’    val instant = this
524â†’    return format.format { setDateTimeOffset(instant, offset) }
525â†’}
526â†’
527â†’internal const val DISTANT_PAST_SECONDS = -3217862419201
528â†’internal const val DISTANT_FUTURE_SECONDS = 3093527980800
529â†’

1â†’# kotlinx-datetime
  2â†’
  3â†’[![Kotlin Alpha](https://kotl.in/badges/alpha.svg)](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/components-stability.html)
  4â†’[![JetBrains official project](https://jb.gg/badges/official.svg)](https://confluence.jetbrains.com/display/ALL/JetBrains+on+GitHub) 
  5â†’[![GitHub license](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-Apache%20License%202.0-blue.svg?style=flat)](http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0)
  6â†’[![Maven Central](https://img.shields.io/maven-central/v/org.jetbrains.kotlinx/kotlinx-datetime.svg?label=Maven%20Central)](https://search.maven.org/search?q=g:%22org.jetbrains.kotlinx%22%20AND%20a:%22kotlinx-datetime%22)
  7â†’[![Kotlin](https://img.shields.io/badge/kotlin-1.9.21-blue.svg?logo=kotlin)](http://kotlinlang.org)
  8â†’[![KDoc link](https://img.shields.io/badge/API_reference-KDoc-blue)](https://kotlinlang.org/api/kotlinx-datetime/)
  9â†’[![Slack channel](https://img.shields.io/badge/chat-slack-blue.svg?logo=slack)](https://kotlinlang.slack.com/messages/kotlinx-datetime/)
 10â†’[![TeamCity build](https://img.shields.io/teamcity/build/s/KotlinTools_KotlinxDatetime_Build_All.svg?server=http%3A%2F%2Fteamcity.jetbrains.com)](https://teamcity.jetbrains.com/viewType.html?buildTypeId=KotlinTools_KotlinxDatetime_Build_All&guest=1)
 11â†’
 12â†’A multiplatform Kotlin library for working with date and time.
 13â†’
 14â†’See [Using in your projects](#using-in-your-projects) for the instructions how to setup a dependency in your project.
 15â†’
 16â†’## Design overview
 17â†’
 18â†’There are a few guiding principles in the design of `kotlinx-datetime`. First of all, it is pragmatic, focused
 19â†’on the most common problems developers face every day (pun intended) when working with dates and times. It is not
 20â†’all-encompassing and lacks some domain-specific utilities that special-purpose applications might need.
 21â†’We chose convenience over generality, so the API surface this library provides is as minimal as possible
 22â†’to meet the use-cases.
 23â†’
 24â†’The library puts a clear boundary between the physical time of an instant and the local, time-zone dependent 
 25â†’civil time, consisting of components such as year, month, etc that people use when talking about time. 
 26â†’We intentionally avoid entities in the library that mix both together and could be misused.
 27â†’However, there are convenience operations that take, for example, a physical instant and perform a calendar-based 
 28â†’adjustment (such as adding a month); all such operations
 29â†’explicitly take a time-zone information as parameter to clearly state that their result depends on the civil time-zone
 30â†’rules which are subject to change at any time.
 31â†’
 32â†’The library is based on the ISO 8601 international standard, other ways to represent dates and times are out of
 33â†’its scope. Internationalization (such as locale-specific month and day names) is out the scope, too. 
 34â†’
 35â†’## Types
 36â†’
 37â†’The library provides a basic set of types for working with date and time:
 38â†’
 39â†’- `Instant` to represent a moment on the UTC-SLS time scale;
 40â†’- `Clock` to obtain the current instant;
 41â†’- `LocalDateTime` to represent date and time components without a reference to the particular time zone; 
 42â†’- `LocalDate` to represent the components of date only;
 43â†’- `LocalTime` to represent the components of time only;
 44â†’- `TimeZone` and `FixedOffsetTimeZone` provide time zone information to convert between `Instant` and `LocalDateTime`;
 45â†’- `Month` and `DayOfWeek` enums;
 46â†’- `DateTimePeriod` to represent a difference between two instants decomposed into date and time units;
 47â†’- `DatePeriod` is a subclass of `DateTimePeriod` with zero time components,
 48â†’it represents a difference between two LocalDate values decomposed into date units.
 49â†’- `DateTimeUnit` provides a set of predefined date and time units to use in arithmetic operations on `Instant` and `LocalDate`. 
 50â†’- `UtcOffset` represents the amount of time the local date/time at a particular time zone differs from the date/time at UTC.
 51â†’
 52â†’### Type use-cases
 53â†’
 54â†’Here is some basic advice on how to choose which of the date-carrying types to use in what cases:
 55â†’
 56â†’- Use `Instant` to represent a timestamp of the event that had already happened in the past (like a timestamp of 
 57â†’  a log entry) or will definitely happen in a well-defined instant of time in the future not far away from now 
 58â†’  (like an order confirmation deadline in 1 hour from now).
 59â†’  
 60â†’- Use `LocalDateTime` to represent a time of the event that is scheduled to happen in the far future at a certain 
 61â†’  local time (like a scheduled meeting in a few months from now). You'll have to keep track of the `TimeZone` of 
 62â†’  the scheduled event separately. Try to avoid converting future events to `Instant` in advance, because time-zone 
 63â†’  rules might change unexpectedly in the future. In this [blog post](https://codeblog.jonskeet.uk/2019/03/27/storing-utc-is-not-a-silver-bullet/), you can read more about why it's not always 
 64â†’  a good idea to use `Instant` everywhere.
 65â†’  
 66â†’  Also, use `LocalDateTime` to decode an `Instant` to its local date-time components for display and UIs.
 67â†’  
 68â†’- Use `LocalDate` to represent the date of an event that does not have a specific time associated with it (like a birth date).
 69â†’
 70â†’- Use `LocalTime` to represent the time of an event that does not have a specific date associated with it.
 71â†’ 
 72â†’## Operations
 73â†’
 74â†’With the above types you can get the following operations done.
 75â†’
 76â†’
 77â†’### Getting the current moment of time
 78â†’
 79â†’The current moment of time can be captured with the `Instant` type. 
 80â†’To obtain an `Instant` corresponding to the current moment of time, 
 81â†’use `now()` function of the `Clock` interface:
 82â†’
 83â†’```kotlin
 84â†’val clock: Clock = ...
 85â†’val currentMoment = clock.now()
 86â†’```
 87â†’
 88â†’An instance of `Clock` can be injected through the function/class parameters, 
 89â†’or you can use its default implementation `Clock.System` that represents the system clock:
 90â†’
 91â†’```kotlin
 92â†’val currentMoment = Clock.System.now()
 93â†’```
 94â†’
 95â†’
 96â†’### Converting an instant to local date and time components
 97â†’
 98â†’An `Instant` is just a counter of high resolution time intervals since the beginning of time scale.
 99â†’To get human readable components from an `Instant` value, you need to convert it to the `LocalDateTime`
100â†’type that represents date and time components without a reference to the particular time zone.
101â†’
102â†’The `TimeZone` type provides the rules to convert instants from and to date/time components.
103â†’
104â†’```kotlin
105â†’val currentMoment: Instant = Clock.System.now()
106â†’val datetimeInUtc: LocalDateTime = currentMoment.toLocalDateTime(TimeZone.UTC)
107â†’val datetimeInSystemZone: LocalDateTime = currentMoment.toLocalDateTime(TimeZone.currentSystemDefault())
108â†’```
109â†’
110â†’A `LocalDateTime` instance exposes familiar components of the Gregorian calendar: 
111â†’`year`, `month`, `dayOfMonth`, `hour`, and so on up to `nanosecond`.
112â†’The property `dayOfWeek` shows what weekday that date is,
113â†’and `dayOfYear` shows the day number since the beginning of a year.
114â†’
115â†’
116â†’Additional time zones can be acquired by their string identifier with the `TimeZone.of(id: String)` function.
117â†’```kotlin
118â†’val tzBerlin = TimeZone.of("Europe/Berlin")
119â†’val datetimeInBerlin = currentMoment.toLocalDateTime(tzBerlin)
120â†’```
121â†’
122â†’A `LocalDateTime` instance can be constructed from individual components:
123â†’
124â†’```kotlin
125â†’val kotlinReleaseDateTime = LocalDateTime(2016, 2, 15, 16, 57, 0, 0)
126â†’```
127â†’
128â†’An instant can be obtained from `LocalDateTime` by interpreting it as a time moment
129â†’in a particular `TimeZone`:
130â†’
131â†’```kotlin
132â†’val kotlinReleaseInstant = kotlinReleaseDateTime.toInstant(TimeZone.of("UTC+3"))
133â†’```
134â†’
135â†’### Getting local date components
136â†’
137â†’A `LocalDate` represents a local date without time. You can obtain one from an `Instant`
138â†’by converting it to `LocalDateTime` and taking its `date` property.
139â†’
140â†’```kotlin
141â†’val now: Instant = Clock.System.now()
142â†’val today: LocalDate = now.toLocalDateTime(TimeZone.currentSystemDefault()).date
143â†’// or shorter
144â†’val today: LocalDate = Clock.System.todayIn(TimeZone.currentSystemDefault())
145â†’```
146â†’Note, that today's date really depends on the time zone in which you're observing the current moment.
147â†’
148â†’`LocalDate` can be constructed from three components, year, month, and day:
149â†’```kotlin
150â†’val knownDate = LocalDate(2020, 2, 21)
151â†’```
152â†’
153â†’### Getting local time components
154â†’
155â†’A `LocalTime` represents local time without date. You can obtain one from an `Instant`
156â†’by converting it to `LocalDateTime` and taking its `time` property.
157â†’
158â†’```kotlin
159â†’val now: Instant = Clock.System.now()
160â†’val thisTime: LocalTime = now.toLocalDateTime(TimeZone.currentSystemDefault()).time
161â†’```
162â†’
163â†’A `LocalTime` can be constructed from four components, hour, minute, second and nanosecond:
164â†’```kotlin
165â†’val knownTime = LocalTime(hour = 23, minute = 59, second = 12)
166â†’val timeWithNanos = LocalTime(hour = 23, minute = 59, second = 12, nanosecond = 999)
167â†’val hourMinute = LocalTime(hour = 12, minute = 13)
168â†’```
169â†’
170â†’### Converting instant to and from unix time
171â†’
172â†’An `Instant` can be converted to a number of milliseconds since the Unix/POSIX epoch with the `toEpochMilliseconds()` function.
173â†’To convert back, use the companion object function `Instant.fromEpochMilliseconds(Long)`.
174â†’
175â†’### Converting instant and local date/time to and from the ISO 8601 string
176â†’
177â†’`Instant`, `LocalDateTime`, `LocalDate` and `LocalTime` provide shortcuts for
178â†’parsing and formatting them using the extended ISO-8601 format.
179â†’The `toString()` function is used to convert the value to a string in that format, and 
180â†’the `parse` function in companion object is used to parse a string representation back. 
181â†’
182â†’```kotlin
183â†’val instantNow = Clock.System.now()
184â†’instantNow.toString()  // returns something like 2015-12-31T12:30:00Z
185â†’val instantBefore = Instant.parse("2010-06-01T22:19:44.475Z")
186â†’```
187â†’
188â†’`LocalDateTime` uses a similar format, but without `Z` UTC time zone designator in the end.
189â†’
190â†’`LocalDate` uses a format with just year, month, and date components, e.g. `2010-06-01`.
191â†’
192â†’`LocalTime` uses a format with just hour, minute, second and (if non-zero) nanosecond components, e.g. `12:01:03`.
193â†’
194â†’```kotlin
195â†’LocalDateTime.parse("2010-06-01T22:19:44")
196â†’LocalDate.parse("2010-06-01")
197â†’LocalTime.parse("12:01:03")
198â†’LocalTime.parse("12:00:03.999")
199â†’LocalTime.parse("12:0:03.999") // fails with an IllegalArgumentException
200â†’```
201â†’
202â†’### Working with other string formats
203â†’
204â†’When some data needs to be formatted in some format other than ISO-8601, one
205â†’can define their own format or use some of the predefined ones:
206â†’
207â†’```kotlin
208â†’// import kotlinx.datetime.format.*
209â†’
210â†’val dateFormat = LocalDate.Format {
211â†’    monthNumber(padding = Padding.SPACE)
212â†’    char('/')
213â†’    dayOfMonth()
214â†’    char(' ')
215â†’    year()
216â†’}
217â†’
218â†’val date = dateFormat.parse("12/24 2023")
219â†’println(date.format(LocalDate.Formats.ISO_BASIC)) // "20231224"
220â†’```
221â†’
222â†’#### Using Unicode format strings (like `yyyy-MM-dd`)
223â†’
224â†’Given a constant format string like the ones used by Java's
225â†’[DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern](https://docs.oracle.com/javase/8/docs/api/java/time/format/DateTimeFormatter.html) can be
226â†’converted to Kotlin code using the following invocation:
227â†’
228â†’```kotlin
229â†’// import kotlinx.datetime.format.*
230â†’
231â†’println(DateTimeFormat.formatAsKotlinBuilderDsl(DateTimeComponents.Format {
232â†’    byUnicodePattern("uuuu-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss[.SSS]Z")
233â†’}))
234â†’
235â†’// will print:
236â†’/*
237â†’date(LocalDate.Formats.ISO)
238â†’char('T')
239â†’hour()
240â†’char(':')
241â†’minute()
242â†’char(':')
243â†’second()
244â†’alternativeParsing({
245â†’}) {
246â†’    char('.')
247â†’    secondFraction(3)
248â†’}
249â†’offset(UtcOffset.Formats.FOUR_DIGITS)
250â†’ */
251â†’```
252â†’
253â†’When your format string is not constant, with the `FormatStringsInDatetimeFormats` opt-in,
254â†’you can use the format without converting it to Kotlin code beforehand:
255â†’
256â†’```kotlin
257â†’val formatPattern = "yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss[.SSS]"
258â†’
259â†’@OptIn(FormatStringsInDatetimeFormats::class)
260â†’val dateTimeFormat = LocalDateTime.Format {
261â†’    byUnicodePattern(formatPattern)
262â†’}
263â†’
264â†’dateTimeFormat.parse("2023-12-24T23:59:59")
265â†’```
266â†’
267â†’### Parsing and formatting partial, compound or out-of-bounds data
268â†’
269â†’Sometimes, the required string format doesn't fully correspond to any of the
270â†’classes `kotlinx-datetime` provides. In these cases, `DateTimeComponents`, a
271â†’collection of all date-time fields, can be used instead.
272â†’
273â†’```kotlin
274â†’// import kotlinx.datetime.format.*
275â†’
276â†’val yearMonth = DateTimeComponents.Format { year(); char('-'); monthNumber() }
277â†’    .parse("2024-01")
278â†’println(yearMonth.year)
279â†’println(yearMonth.monthNumber)
280â†’
281â†’val dateTimeOffset = DateTimeComponents.Formats.ISO_DATE_TIME_OFFSET
282â†’    .parse("2023-01-07T23:16:15.53+02:00")
283â†’println(dateTimeOffset.toUtcOffset()) // +02:00
284â†’println(dateTimeOffset.toLocalDateTime()) // 2023-01-07T23:16:15.53
285â†’```
286â†’
287â†’Occasionally, one can encounter strings where the values are slightly off:
288â†’for example, `23:59:60`, where `60` is an invalid value for the second.
289â†’`DateTimeComponents` allows parsing such values as well and then mutating them
290â†’before conversion.
291â†’
292â†’```kotlin
293â†’val time = DateTimeComponents.Format { time(LocalTime.Formats.ISO) }
294â†’    .parse("23:59:60").apply {
295â†’        if (second == 60) second = 59
296â†’    }.toLocalTime()
297â†’println(time) // 23:59:59
298â†’```
299â†’
300â†’Because `DateTimeComponents` is provided specifically for parsing and
301â†’formatting, there is no way to construct it normally. If one needs to format
302â†’partial, complex or out-of-bounds data, the `format` function allows building
303â†’`DateTimeComponents` specifically for formatting it:
304â†’
305â†’```kotlin
306â†’DateTimeComponents.Formats.RFC_1123.format {
307â†’    // the receiver of this lambda is DateTimeComponents
308â†’    setDate(LocalDate(2023, 1, 7))
309â†’    hour = 23
310â†’    minute = 59
311â†’    second = 60
312â†’    setOffset(UtcOffset(hours = 2))
313â†’} // Sat, 7 Jan 2023 23:59:60 +0200
314â†’```
315â†’
316â†’### Instant arithmetic
317â†’
318â†’```kotlin
319â†’val now = Clock.System.now()
320â†’val instantInThePast: Instant = Instant.parse("2020-01-01T00:00:00Z")
321â†’val durationSinceThen: Duration = now - instantInThePast
322â†’val equidistantInstantInTheFuture: Instant = now + durationSinceThen
323â†’```
324â†’
325â†’`Duration` is a type from the experimental `kotlin.time` package in the Kotlin standard library.
326â†’This type holds the amount of time that can be represented in different time units: from nanoseconds to 24H days.
327â†’
328â†’To get the calendar difference between two instants you can use the `Instant.periodUntil(Instant, TimeZone)` function.
329â†’
330â†’```kotlin
331â†’val period: DateTimePeriod = instantInThePast.periodUntil(Clock.System.now(), TimeZone.UTC)
332â†’```
333â†’
334â†’A `DateTimePeriod` represents a difference between two particular moments as a sum of calendar components, 
335â†’like "2 years, 3 months, 10 days, and 22 hours".
336â†’
337â†’The difference can be calculated as an integer amount of specified date or time units:
338â†’
339â†’```kotlin
340â†’val diffInMonths = instantInThePast.until(Clock.System.now(), DateTimeUnit.MONTH, TimeZone.UTC)
341â†’```
342â†’There are also shortcuts `yearsUntil(...)`, `monthsUntil(...)`, and `daysUntil(...)`.
343â†’
344â†’A particular amount of date/time units or a date/time period can be added to an `Instant` with the `plus` function:
345â†’
346â†’```kotlin
347â†’val now = Clock.System.now()
348â†’val systemTZ = TimeZone.currentSystemDefault()
349â†’val tomorrow = now.plus(2, DateTimeUnit.DAY, systemTZ)
350â†’val threeYearsAndAMonthLater = now.plus(DateTimePeriod(years = 3, months = 1), systemTZ)
351â†’```
352â†’
353â†’Note that `plus` and `...until` operations require a `TimeZone` as a parameter because the calendar interval between 
354â†’two particular instants can be different, when calculated in different time zones.
355â†’
356â†’### Date arithmetic
357â†’
358â†’Similar operations with date units are provided for `LocalDate` type:
359â†’
360â†’- `LocalDate.plus(number, DateTimeUnit.DateBased)`
361â†’- `LocalDate.plus(DatePeriod)`
362â†’- `LocalDate.until(LocalDate, DateTimeUnit.DateBased)` and the shortcuts `yearsUntil`, `monthUntil`, `daysUntil`
363â†’- `LocalDate.periodUntil(LocalDate): DatePeriod` and `LocalDate.minus(LocalDate): DatePeriod`
364â†’
365â†’Notice that, instead of the general `DateTimeUnit` and `DateTimePeriod` types, we're using their subtypes
366â†’`DateTimeUnit.DateBased` and `DatePeriod` respectively. This allows preventing the situations when
367â†’time components are being added to a date at compile time.
368â†’
369â†’### Date + time arithmetic
370â†’
371â†’Arithmetic on `LocalDateTime` is intentionally omitted. The reason for this is that the presence of daylight saving time
372â†’transitions (changing from standard time to daylight saving time and back) causes `LocalDateTime` arithmetic to be
373â†’ill-defined. For example, consider time gaps (or, as [`dst` tag wiki on Stack Overflow](https://stackoverflow.com/tags/dst/info)
374â†’calls them, "spring forward" transitions), that is, ranges of date + time combinations that never occur in a given
375â†’time zone due to clocks moving forward. If we allowed `LocalDateTime` arithmetic that ignored time zones, then it
376â†’could result in `LocalDateTime` instances that are inside a time gap and are invalid in the implied time zone.
377â†’
378â†’Therefore, the recommended way to use a `LocalDateTime` is to treat it as a representation of an `Instant`,
379â†’perform all the required arithmetic on `Instant` values, and only convert to `LocalDateTime` when a human-readable
380â†’representation is needed.
381â†’
382â†’```kotlin
383â†’val timeZone = TimeZone.of("Europe/Berlin")
384â†’val localDateTime = LocalDateTime.parse("2021-03-27T02:16:20")
385â†’val instant = localDateTime.toInstant(timeZone)
386â†’
387â†’val instantOneDayLater = instant.plus(1, DateTimeUnit.DAY, timeZone)
388â†’val localDateTimeOneDayLater = instantOneDayLater.toLocalDateTime(timeZone)
389â†’// 2021-03-28T03:16:20, as 02:16:20 that day is in a time gap
390â†’
391â†’val instantTwoDaysLater = instant.plus(2, DateTimeUnit.DAY, timeZone)
392â†’val localDateTimeTwoDaysLater = instantTwoDaysLater.toLocalDateTime(timeZone)
393â†’// 2021-03-29T02:16:20
394â†’```
395â†’
396â†’## Implementation
397â†’
398â†’The implementation of date/time types, such as `Instant`, `LocalDateTime`, `TimeZone` and so on, relies on:
399â†’
400â†’- in JVM: [`java.time`](https://docs.oracle.com/javase/8/docs/api/java/time/package-summary.html) API;
401â†’- in Js and Wasm-Js: [`js-joda`](https://js-joda.github.io/js-joda/) library;
402â†’- in Native: based on the [ThreeTen backport project](https://www.threeten.org/threetenbp/)
403â†’  - time zone support is provided by [date](https://github.com/HowardHinnant/date/) C++ library;
404â†’
405â†’## Known/open issues, work TBD
406â†’
407â†’- [x] Some kind of `Clock` interface is needed as a pluggable replacement for `Instant.now()`.
408â†’- [ ] Flexible locale-neutral parsing and formatting facilities are needed to support various date/time interchange
409â†’  formats that are used in practice (in particular, various RFCs).
410â†’
411â†’## Using in your projects
412â†’
413â†’> Note that the library is experimental, and the API is subject to change.
414â†’
415â†’The library is published to Maven Central.
416â†’
417â†’The library is compatible with the Kotlin Standard Library not lower than `1.9.0`.
418â†’
419â†’If you target Android devices running **below API 26**, you need to use Android Gradle plugin 4.0 or newer 
420â†’and enable [core library desugaring](https://developer.android.com/studio/write/java8-support#library-desugaring).
421â†’
422â†’### Gradle
423â†’
424â†’- Add the Maven Central repository if it is not already there:
425â†’
426â†’```kotlin
427â†’repositories {
428â†’    mavenCentral()
429â†’}
430â†’```
431â†’
432â†’- In multiplatform projects, add a dependency to the commonMain source set dependencies
433â†’```kotlin
434â†’kotlin {
435â†’    sourceSets {
436â†’        commonMain {
437â†’             dependencies {
438â†’                 implementation("org.jetbrains.kotlinx:kotlinx-datetime:0.6.0-RC.2")
439â†’             }
440â†’        }
441â†’    }
442â†’}
443â†’```
444â†’
445â†’- To use the library in a single-platform project, add a dependency to the dependencies block.
446â†’
447â†’```groovy
448â†’dependencies {
449â†’    implementation("org.jetbrains.kotlinx:kotlinx-datetime:0.6.0-RC.2")
450â†’}
451â†’```
452â†’
453â†’#### Note about time zones in JS
454â†’
455â†’By default, there's only one time zone available in Kotlin/JS: the `SYSTEM` time zone with a fixed offset.
456â†’
457â†’If you want to use all time zones in Kotlin/JS platform, you need to add the following npm dependency:
458â†’
459â†’```kotlin
460â†’kotlin {
461â†’    sourceSets {
462â†’        val jsMain by getting {
463â†’            dependencies {
464â†’                implementation(npm("@js-joda/timezone", "2.3.0"))
465â†’            }
466â†’        }
467â†’    }
468â†’}
469â†’```
470â†’
471â†’and after that add the following initialization code in your project:
472â†’
473â†’```kotlin
474â†’@JsModule("@js-joda/timezone")
475â†’@JsNonModule
476â†’external object JsJodaTimeZoneModule
477â†’
478â†’private val jsJodaTz = JsJodaTimeZoneModule
479â†’```
480â†’
481â†’### Maven
482â†’
483â†’Add a dependency to the `<dependencies>` element. Note that you need to use the platform-specific `-jvm` artifact in Maven.
484â†’
485â†’```xml
486â†’<dependency>
487â†’    <groupId>org.jetbrains.kotlinx</groupId>
488â†’    <artifactId>kotlinx-datetime-jvm</artifactId>
489â†’    <version>0.6.0-RC.2</version>
490â†’</dependency>
491â†’```
492â†’
493â†’## Building
494â†’
495â†’The project requires JDK 8 to build classes and to run tests. 
496â†’Gradle will try to find it among the installed JDKs or [provision](https://docs.gradle.org/current/userguide/toolchains.html#sec:provisioning) it automatically if it couldn't be found.
497â†’The path to JDK 8 can be additionally specified with the environment variable `JDK_8`. 
498â†’For local builds, you can use a later version of JDK if you don't have that 
499â†’version installed. Specify the version of this JDK with the `java.mainToolchainVersion` Gradle property.
500â†’
501â†’After that, the project can be opened in IDEA and built with Gradle.
502â†’
503â†’For building and running benchmarks, see [README.md](benchmarks/README.md)
504â†’

 * A source of [Instant] values.

 * This interface represents a clock that can provide the current moment in time.

 * by providing a fixed or controllable clock.
 *

 *
 * See [Clock.System][Clock.System] for the clock instance that queries the operating system.
 */
public interface Clock {
    /**
     * Returns the [Instant] corresponding to the current time, according to this clock.
     *
     * The returned [Instant] is expected to be on the UTC-SLS time scale.
     */
    public fun now(): Instant

     * The [Clock] instance that queries the operating system as its source of knowledge of time.
     *
     * On the JVM, this uses `java.time.Clock.systemUTC()`.
     * On JS, this uses `Date.now()`.
     * On Native, this uses `gettimeofday` or `clock_gettime`.
     *
     * The system clock is subject to changes by the operating system, such as NTP updates or manual adjustments.
     * It is not guaranteed to be monotonic.
     */
    public object System : Clock {"}

{"old_string":"/**
 * A moment in time.
 *
 * A point in time must be uniquely identified, so that it is independent of a time zone.
 * For example, `1970-01-01, 00:00:00` does not represent a moment in time, since this would happen at different times
 * in different time zones: someone in Tokyo would think its already `1970-01-01` several hours earlier than someone in
 * Berlin would. To represent such entities, use [LocalDateTime].
 * In contrast, "the moment the clocks in London first showed 00:00 on Jan 1, 2000" is a specific moment
 * in time, as is "1970-01-01, 00:00:00 UTC+0", and so it can be represented as an [Instant].
 *
 * `Instant` uses the UTC-SLS (smeared leap second) time scale. This time scale doesn't contain instants
 * corresponding to leap seconds, but instead "smears" positive and negative leap seconds among the last 1000 seconds
 * of the day when a leap second happens.
 *
 * Some ways in which [Instant] can be acquired are:
 * - [Clock.now] can be used to query the current moment for the given clock. With [Clock.System], it is the current
 *   moment as the platform sees it.
 * - [Instant.parse] parses an ISO-8601 string.
 * - [Instant.fromEpochMilliseconds] and [Instant.fromEpochSeconds] construct the instant values from the amount of time
 *   since `1970-01-01T00:00:00Z` (the Unix epoch).
 */
@Serializable(with = InstantIso8601Serializer::class)
public expect class Instant : Comparable<Instant> {","file_path":"core/common/src/Instant.kt","new_string":"/**
 * A moment in time.
 *
 * A point in time must be uniquely identified, so that it is independent of a time zone.
 * For example, `1970-01-01, 00:00:00` does not represent a moment in time, since this would happen at different times
 * in different time zones: someone in Tokyo would think its already `1970-01-01` several hours earlier than someone in
 * Berlin would. To represent such entities, use [LocalDateTime].
 * In contrast, "the moment the clocks in London first showed 00:00 on Jan 1, 2000" is a specific moment
 * in time, as is "1970-01-01, 00:00:00 UTC+0", and so it can be represented as an [Instant].
 *
 * `Instant` uses the UTC-SLS (smeared leap second) time scale. This time scale doesn't contain instants
 * corresponding to leap seconds, but instead "smears" positive and negative leap seconds among the last 1000 seconds
 * of the day when a leap second happens.
 *
 * Some ways in which [Instant] can be acquired are:
 * - [Clock.now] can be used to query the current moment for the given clock. With [Clock.System], it is the current
 *   moment as the platform sees it.
 * - [Instant.parse] parses an ISO-8601 string.
 * - [Instant.fromEpochMilliseconds] and [Instant.fromEpochSeconds] construct the instant values from the amount of time
 *   since `1970-01-01T00:00:00Z` (the Unix epoch).
 *
 * Basic usage example:
 * ```
 * val now = Clock.System.now()
 * val instantInThePast = Instant.parse("2020-01-01T00:00:00Z")
 * val durationSinceThen = now - instantInThePast
 * println("Time elapsed since 2020: $durationSinceThen")
 * ```
 *
 * Gotchas and recommendations:
 * - `instant + duration` is a physical time operation, not a calendar operation. For example, `instant + 1.days`
 *   always adds exactly 86,400 seconds, which might not be what you want if you're dealing with daylight saving time.
 *   To add a calendar day, use `instant.plus(1, DateTimeUnit.DAY, timeZone)`.
 * - `Instant` values are limited to a range that is approximately -100,000 to +100,000 years around the epoch.
 *   The exact range depends on the platform, but it is guaranteed to be at least [DISTANT_PAST] to [DISTANT_FUTURE].
 *
 * On the JVM, `kotlinx.datetime.Instant` is equivalent to `java.time.Instant`.
 * Conversion functions `toJavaInstant()` and `toKotlinInstant()` are available for interoperability.
 */
@Serializable(with = InstantIso8601Serializer::class)
public expect class Instant : Comparable<Instant> {"}
